data.text: I'll start by exploring the codebase to understand the current structure before implementing the passthrough envelope.

input.prompt:
Search the repository at /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-20047dd5-20260430T155920-cdb15a42 for the agent execution code. I need to understand:

1. Where AgentPassthrough or AgentConstraints types are defined (or if they need to be created)
2. The ServiceExecuteRequest struct definition
3. How --harness, --provider, --model CLI flags are handled in cmd/
4. The execute-loop / work command flow
5. Where retry/MinPower/MaxPower logic lives
6. Where run evidence is written

Look in cli/cmd/ for agent run, try, work commands, and cli/internal/ for agent-related packages. Find all files that reference harness, provider, model in the context of agent execution. Report file paths and key struct/function names.

1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"fmt"
6		"os"
7		"path/filepath"
8		"regexp"
9	
10		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent"
11		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
12		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"
13		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
14	)
15	
16	var validBeadID = regexp.MustCompile(`^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$`)
17	
18	// loadExecutionsMirrorConfig reads .ddx/config.yaml at projectRoot and returns
19	// the executions.mirror block when configured. Errors return nil silently —
20	// missing or invalid mirror config simply disables mirroring; it never blocks
21	// execute-bead.
22	func loadExecutionsMirrorConfig(projectRoot string) *config.ExecutionsMirrorConfig {
23		cfg, err := config.LoadWithWorkingDir(projectRoot)
24		if err != nil || cfg == nil || cfg.Executions == nil || cfg.Executions.Mirror == nil {
25			return nil
26		}
27		return cfg.Executions.Mirror
28	}
29	
30	// landingGitOpsFromFactory returns the agent.LandingGitOps the CommandFactory
31	// should use — either the test override or the default RealLandingGitOps.
32	func landingGitOpsFromFactory(f *CommandFactory) agent.LandingGitOps {
33		if f.executeBeadLandingGitOverride != nil {
34			return f.executeBeadLandingGitOverride
35		}
36		return agent.RealLandingGitOps{}
37	}
38	
39	func (f *CommandFactory) newAgentExecuteBeadCommand() *cobra.Command {
40		cmd := &cobra.Command{
41			Use:   "execute-bead <bead-id>",
42			Short: "Run an agent on one bead in an isolated worktree, then land the result",
43			Long: `execute-bead is the primitive: it runs a single agent on a single bead in
44	an isolated git worktree (the worker step), then hands the result to the
45	parent-side orchestrator which merges or preserves the commits.
46	
47	The worker classifies its outcome as one of:
48	  task_succeeded  — agent exited 0 and produced commits
49	  task_no_changes — agent exited 0 but made no commits
50	  task_failed     — agent exited non-zero
51	
52	The orchestrator then applies landing logic (merge, gate evaluation, preserve)
53	and the final result reflects both the worker and landing decisions.
54	
55	For normal queue-driven work use "ddx agent execute-loop", which claims
56	ready beads, calls this command, and closes or unclaims each bead based on
57	the result. Reach for execute-bead directly only to debug or re-run a
58	specific bead.
59	
60	Result status is reported on the "status:" line and is one of:
61	  success                          — merged (or preserved with --no-merge)
62	  no_changes                       — agent ran but produced no diff
63	  no_evidence_produced             — agent exited with no commit and no rationale
64	  already_satisfied                — closed by the loop after repeated no_changes
65	  land_conflict                    — merge failed; result preserved
66	  post_run_check_failed            — checks failed; result preserved
67	  execution_failed                 — agent or harness error
68	  structural_validation_failed     — bead or prompt inputs invalid
69	
70	execute-loop closes the bead with session/commit evidence only on success
71	(and already_satisfied); every other status leaves the bead open and
72	unclaimed for a later attempt.`,
73			Example: `  # Debug one specific bead (prefer "execute-loop" for normal queue work)
74	  ddx agent execute-bead ddx-7a01ba6c
75	
76	  # Run against a non-HEAD base revision
77	  ddx agent execute-bead ddx-7a01ba6c --from main
78	
79	  # Preserve the result under refs/ddx/iterations/... instead of merging
80	  ddx agent execute-bead ddx-7a01ba6c --no-merge
81	
82	  # Pin harness/model for a debugging pass
83	  ddx agent execute-bead ddx-7a01ba6c --harness codex`,
84			Args: cobra.ExactArgs(1),

104	func (f *CommandFactory) runAgentExecuteBead(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
105		beadID := args[0]
106		if !validBeadID.MatchString(beadID) {
107			return fmt.Errorf("invalid bead ID %q: must contain only letters, digits, dots, underscores, and hyphens", beadID)
108		}
109		projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
110		fromRev, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("from")
111		noMerge, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-merge")
112		harness, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("harness")
113		model, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model")
114		provider, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("provider")
115		modelRef, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model-ref")
116		effort, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("effort")
117		contextBudget, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("context-budget")
118		promptFile, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("prompt")
119		requestTimeout, _ := cmd.Flags().GetDuration("request-timeout")
120		minPower, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("min-power")
121		maxPower, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("max-power")
122		asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
123	
124		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
125	
126		overrides := config.CLIOverrides{
127			Harness:       harness,
128			Model:         model,
129			Provider:      provider,
130			ModelRef:      modelRef,
131			Effort:        effort,
132			ContextBudget: contextBudget,
133			MinPower:      minPower,
134			MaxPower:      maxPower,
135		}
136		if requestTimeout > 0 {
137			overrides.ProviderRequestTimeout = &requestTimeout
138		}
139		rcfg, err := config.LoadAndResolve(projectRoot, overrides)
140		if err != nil {
141			return fmt.Errorf("load config at %s: %w", projectRoot, err)
142		}
143		runtime := agent.ExecuteBeadRuntime{
144			FromRev:    fromRev,
145			PromptFile: promptFile,
146			WorkerID:   os.Getenv("DDX_WORKER_ID"),
147			BeadEvents: bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")),
148		}
149	
150		var gitOps agent.GitOps = &agent.RealGitOps{}
151		if f.executeBeadGitOverride != nil {
152			gitOps = f.executeBeadGitOverride
153		}
154	
155		var orchestratorGitOps agent.OrchestratorGitOps = &agent.RealOrchestratorGitOps{}
156		if f.executeBeadOrchestratorGitOverride != nil {
157			orchestratorGitOps = f.executeBeadOrchestratorGitOverride
158		}
159	
160		// Test injection seam: AgentRunnerOverride feeds canned *Result values
161		// from fakes without spinning up a real provider. Production callers
162		// leave this nil so ExecuteBead constructs an agent service from
163		// projectRoot internally.
164		if f.AgentRunnerOverride != nil {
165			runtime.AgentRunner = f.AgentRunnerOverride
166		}
167	
168		// Preflight the orphan-model check before creating a worktree. Mirrors
169		// the execute-loop preflight so `ddx agent execute-bead --model <unroutable>`
170		// errors before any git work happens rather than mid-execution. Skip the
171		// preflight when a test runner override is in use — overrides may be
172		// fakes that bypass routing entirely.
173		if f.AgentRunnerOverride == nil {
174			if err := agent.ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService(cmd.Context(), f.WorkingDir, harness, model, provider, modelRef); err != nil {
175				return err
176			}
177		}
178	
179		// Recover any orphaned worktrees from previous crashed runs before
180		// spawning a new worker. This is the parent's responsibility.
181		agent.RecoverOrphans(gitOps, projectRoot, beadID)
182	
183		// Worker step: run the agent in an isolated worktree.
184		res, err := agent.ExecuteBeadWithConfig(cmd.Context(), projectRoot, beadID, rcfg, runtime, gitOps)
185		if err != nil && res == nil {
186			return err
187		}
188	
189		// Orchestrator step: land the result (ff or merge → push, gate eval,
190		// preserve). Always run when we have a result — the orchestrator handles
191		// all cases including no-changes and error (agent failed with no commits).
192		//
193		// Single-bead CLI is strictly serial (no sibling workers in this
194		// process), so we can wire the LandingAdvancer to call Land() directly
195		// without going through a per-project coordinator goroutine. Tests may
196		// inject a custom advancer via f.executeBeadLandingAdvancerOverride.
197		if res != nil {
198			var advancer func(r *agent.ExecuteBeadResult) (*agent.LandResult, error)
199			if f.executeBeadLandingAdvancerOverride != nil {
200				advancer = f.executeBeadLandingAdvancerOverride
201			} else {
202				landingGitOps := landingGitOpsFromFactory(f)
203				advancer = func(r *agent.ExecuteBeadResult) (*agent.LandResult, error) {
204					return agent.Land(projectRoot, agent.BuildLandRequestFromResult(projectRoot, r), landingGitOps)
205				}
206			}
207			landingOpts := agent.BeadLandingOptions{
208				NoMerge:         noMerge,
209				LandingAdvancer: advancer,
210			}
211	
212			// Wire required execution gates: when the worker manifest declares
213			// governing IDs, gate-eval them in an ephemeral worktree at ResultRev
214			// before LandBeadResult decides merge vs preserve. The original
215			// worker worktree was cleaned up by ExecuteBead, so we pin ResultRev
216			// to a transient ref and check it out into a temp worktree for the
217			// duration of gate evaluation.
218			if res != nil && res.ResultRev != "" && res.ResultRev != res.BaseRev && res.ExitCode == 0 {
219				wt, ids, cleanup, ctxErr := agent.BuildLandingGateContext(projectRoot, res, gitOps)
220				if ctxErr != nil {
221					// Soft-fail: log and skip gate eval rather than abort the land.
222					fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "ddx: warning: gate-context setup failed: %v (skipping required-gate eval)\n", ctxErr)
223				} else if wt != "" {
224					defer cleanup()
225					landingOpts.WtPath = wt
226					landingOpts.GovernIDs = ids
227					landingOpts.ChecksArtifactPath = filepath.Join(projectRoot, res.ExecutionDir, "checks.json")
228					landingOpts.ChecksArtifactRel = filepath.Join(res.ExecutionDir, "checks.json")
229				}
230			}
231	
232			if landing, landErr := agent.LandBeadResult(projectRoot, res, orchestratorGitOps, landingOpts); landErr == nil {
233				agent.ApplyLandingToResult(res, landing)
234			} else if err == nil {
235				err = landErr
236			}
237		}
238	
239		if err != nil {
240			_ = writeExecuteBeadResult(cmd, res, asJSON)
241			return err
242		}
243	
244		return writeExecuteBeadResult(cmd, res, asJSON)
245	}
246	
247	func writeExecuteBeadResult(cmd *cobra.Command, res *agent.ExecuteBeadResult, asJSON bool) error {
248		if res == nil {
249			return nil
250		}
251		if asJSON {
252			enc := json.NewEncoder(cmd.OutOrStdout())
253			enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
254			return enc.Encode(res)
255		}
256	
257		fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "bead:    %s\n", res.BeadID)
258		fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "base:    %s\n", res.BaseRev)
259		if res.ResultRev != "" && res.ResultRev != res.BaseRev {
260			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "result:  %s\n", res.ResultRev)
261		}
262		fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "outcome: %s\n", res.Outcome)
263		fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "status:  %s\n", res.Status)
264		if res.Detail != "" {
265			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "detail:  %s\n", res.Detail)
266		}
267		if res.NoChangesRationale != "" {
268			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "rationale: %s\n", res.NoChangesRationale)
269		}
270		if res.PreserveRef != "" {
271			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "ref:     %s\n", res.PreserveRef)
272		}
273		return nil
274	}

9	// CLIOverrides carries per-invocation flag values that override
10	// project config. Zero values mean "no override; use config".
11	// Pointer fields distinguish "explicit value" from "not set".
12	type CLIOverrides struct {
13		Harness       string
14		Model         string
15		Provider      string
16		ModelRef      string
17		Profile       string
18		Effort        string
19		Permissions   string
20		MinTier       string
21		MaxTier       string
22		Timeout       *time.Duration
23		NoReview      *bool
24		Assignee      string
25		ContextBudget string
26		// ProviderRequestTimeout, when non-nil, overrides the per-request wall-clock
27		// cap applied to a single Chat/ChatStream call. Corresponds to --request-timeout
28		// on execute-bead and execute-loop. Zero pointer means "use config or model default".
29		ProviderRequestTimeout *time.Duration
30		// MinPower and MaxPower are passthrough power-bounds for the upstream agent
31		// routing contract (CONTRACT-003 / FEAT-006). DDx passes these to
32		// ServiceExecuteRequest unchanged; the agent owns model selection within the
33		// bounds. Zero means no bound (unconstrained). Corresponds to --min-power and
34		// --max-power on execute-bead and execute-loop.
35		MinPower int
36		MaxPower int
37	}

39	// Resolve produces a sealed ResolvedConfig by layering overrides onto
40	// cfg. Overrides are applied as last-write-wins per field. The
41	// returned ResolvedConfig does not alias cfg's storage — every map
42	// and slice is deep-cloned via per-type Clone methods before being
43	// captured.
44	//
45	// Resolve accepts a nil cfg and returns a ResolvedConfig populated
46	// from package defaults, sealed normally.
47	func (c *NewConfig) Resolve(overrides CLIOverrides) ResolvedConfig {
48		r := ResolvedConfig{sealed: true}
49	
50		r.assignee = overrides.Assignee
51		r.reviewMaxRetries = c.ResolveReviewMaxRetries()
52	
53		var agent *AgentConfig
54		var workers *WorkersConfig
55		if c != nil {
56			agent = c.Agent.Clone()
57			workers = c.Workers
58		}
59	
60		r.noProgressCooldown = workers.ResolveNoProgressCooldown()
61		r.maxNoChangesBeforeClose = workers.ResolveMaxNoChangesBeforeClose()
62		r.heartbeatInterval = workers.ResolveHeartbeatInterval()
63	
64		r.harness = overrides.Harness
65		if r.harness == "" && agent != nil {
66			r.harness = agent.Harness
67		}
68	
69		r.model = overrides.Model
70		if r.model == "" && agent != nil {
71			r.model = agent.Model
72		}
73	
74		r.provider = overrides.Provider
75		r.modelRef = overrides.ModelRef
76		r.profile = overrides.Profile
77		r.minTier = overrides.MinTier
78		r.maxTier = overrides.MaxTier
79		r.effort = overrides.Effort
80		r.minPower = overrides.MinPower
81		r.maxPower = overrides.MaxPower
82	
83		r.permissions = overrides.Permissions
84		if r.permissions == "" && agent != nil {
85			r.permissions = agent.Permissions
86		}
87	
88		if overrides.Timeout != nil {
89			r.timeout = *overrides.Timeout
90		} else if agent != nil && agent.TimeoutMS > 0 {
91			r.timeout = time.Duration(agent.TimeoutMS) * time.Millisecond
92		}
93	
94		if overrides.ProviderRequestTimeout != nil {
95			r.providerRequestTimeout = *overrides.ProviderRequestTimeout
96		}
97	
98		r.evidenceCaps = c.ResolveEvidenceCaps(r.harness)
99	
100		r.contextBudget = overrides.ContextBudget
101		if r.contextBudget == "" && c != nil {
102			r.contextBudget = c.EvidenceCaps.ResolveContextBudget()
103		}
104	
105		if agent != nil {
106			r.sessionLogDir = agent.SessionLogDir
107			if agent.ReasoningLevels != nil {
108				r.reasoningLevels = make(map[string][]string, len(agent.ReasoningLevels))
109				for k, v := range agent.ReasoningLevels {
110					r.reasoningLevels[k] = append([]string(nil), v...)
111				}
112			}
113		}
114		if c != nil && c.Executions != nil && c.Executions.Mirror != nil {
115			r.mirrorConfig = c.Executions.Mirror.Clone()
116		}
117	
118		return r
119	}

121	// LoadAndResolve is the canonical production entry point that produces
122	// a sealed ResolvedConfig. It loads the project's .ddx/config.yaml from
123	// projectRoot, layers overrides on top, and returns the immutable
124	// ResolvedConfig.
125	//
126	// On load error, LoadAndResolve still returns a usable, sealed
127	// ResolvedConfig populated from package defaults plus the supplied
128	// overrides, alongside the underlying error. Callers decide whether to
129	// surface the error or proceed with the defaults-backed config.
130	//
131	// If projectRoot is empty, the process working directory is used.
132	func LoadAndResolve(projectRoot string, overrides CLIOverrides) (ResolvedConfig, error) {
133		cfg, err := LoadWithWorkingDir(projectRoot)
134		if err != nil {
135			return DefaultNewConfig().Resolve(overrides), err
136		}
137		return cfg.Resolve(overrides), nil
138	}
139	
140	// ResolvedConfig is the loop/runner/reviewer's view of merged project
141	// config plus per-invocation overrides. It is constructed only by
142	// (*Config).Resolve / config.LoadAndResolve and is safe to share across
143	// goroutines (no method mutates it).
144	//
145	// Sealed-construction: every public accessor calls requireSealed on
146	// entry. A zero-value ResolvedConfig{} or `var r ResolvedConfig` is a
147	// valid Go expression but fails loudly on first read with a panic
148	// naming LoadAndResolve. See SD-024 / TD-024.
149	type ResolvedConfig struct {
150		sealed bool
151	
152		assignee                string
153		reviewMaxRetries        int
154		noProgressCooldown      time.Duration
155		maxNoChangesBeforeClose int
156		heartbeatInterval       time.Duration
157		harness                 string
158		model                   string
159		provider                string
160		modelRef                string
161		profile                 string
162		minTier                 string
163		maxTier                 string
164		effort                  string
165		minPower                int
166		maxPower                int
167		permissions             string
168		timeout                 time.Duration
169		wallClock               time.Duration
170		contextBudget           string
171		evidenceCaps            evidence.Caps
172		sessionLogDir           string
173		mirrorConfig            *ExecutionsMirrorConfig
174		reasoningLevels         map[string][]string
175		providerRequestTimeout  time.Duration
176	}
177	
178	// requireSealed panics if r was not produced by Resolve / LoadAndResolve.
179	// Called as the first statement of every public accessor.
180	func (r ResolvedConfig) requireSealed() {
181		if !r.sealed {
182			panic("config: ResolvedConfig used without going through " +
183				"(*Config).Resolve or config.LoadAndResolve. " +
184				"Production callers must obtain a ResolvedConfig from " +
185				"LoadAndResolve; tests must use NewTestConfigFor*.")
186		}
187	}
188	
189	func (r ResolvedConfig) Assignee() string {
190		r.requireSealed()
191		return r.assignee
192	}
193	
194	func (r ResolvedConfig) ReviewMaxRetries() int {
195		r.requireSealed()
196		return r.reviewMaxRetries
197	}

30	// Routing test seams (ddx-755f5881 AC #2): record every RouteRequest issued
31	// by the default `ddx agent run` path so unit tests can assert that the
32	// default path calls ResolveRoute exactly once with Profile: "default" and
33	// no other fields. Production code never reads this slice; it is exported
34	// only for tests.
35	var (
36		routeRequestRecorderMu sync.Mutex
37		routeRequestRecorder   []agentlib.RouteRequest
38	)
39	
40	func recordRouteRequestForTest(req agentlib.RouteRequest) {
41		routeRequestRecorderMu.Lock()
42		routeRequestRecorder = append(routeRequestRecorder, req)
43		routeRequestRecorderMu.Unlock()
44	}
45	
46	// RecordedRouteRequestsForTest returns a copy of all RouteRequests recorded
47	// by the agent CLI surface since the last reset.
48	func RecordedRouteRequestsForTest() []agentlib.RouteRequest {
49		routeRequestRecorderMu.Lock()
50		defer routeRequestRecorderMu.Unlock()
51		out := make([]agentlib.RouteRequest, len(routeRequestRecorder))
52		copy(out, routeRequestRecorder)
53		return out
54	}
55	
56	// ResetRecordedRouteRequestsForTest clears the route-request recording slice.
57	func ResetRecordedRouteRequestsForTest() {
58		routeRequestRecorderMu.Lock()
59		routeRequestRecorder = nil
60		routeRequestRecorderMu.Unlock()
61	}

71	func SetAgentServiceFactoryForTest(f func(workDir string) (agentlib.DdxAgent, error)) {

85	func (f *CommandFactory) newAgentCommand() *cobra.Command {
86		cmd := &cobra.Command{
87			Use:   "agent",
88			Short: "Invoke AI agents with harness dispatch, quorum, and session logging",
89			Long: `Unified interface for dispatching work to AI coding agents.
90	
91	Supports multiple harnesses (codex, claude, gemini, etc.) with output capture,
92	token tracking, session logging, and multi-agent quorum.
93	
94	The embedded DDx agent harness is named 'agent' and is always available without
95	installing external binaries. Use --harness agent or --profile cheap to route to it.
96	
97	Profile routing (--profile default|cheap|fast|smart) selects the best available harness
98	and model automatically. Workflow tools should prefer --profile over --harness to
99	stay decoupled from harness installation details.
100	
101	Examples:
102	  ddx agent run --profile default --prompt task.md
103	  ddx agent run --profile cheap --prompt task.md
104	  ddx agent run --profile smart --prompt task.md
105	  ddx agent run --profile smart --model gpt-5.4   # explicit override; avoid by default
106	  ddx agent run --harness agent --prompt task.md
107	  ddx agent run --harness codex --prompt task.md
108	  ddx agent run --quorum majority --harnesses codex,claude --prompt task.md
109	  ddx agent list
110	  ddx agent capabilities agent
111	  ddx agent capabilities codex
112	  ddx agent doctor

171				var timeout time.Duration

223							if len(parts) >= 3 {

225							} else if len(parts) >= 2 && parts[1] != "" {

227							}
228						}
229					} else if harnesses != "" {

231					} else {
232						return fmt.Errorf("--arm or --harnesses required for --compare mode")
233					}
234	
235					overrides := config.CLIOverrides{
236						Model:       model,
237						Effort:      effort,
238						Permissions: permissions,
239					}
240					if timeout > 0 {
241						overrides.Timeout = &timeout
242					}
243					rcfg, _ := config.LoadAndResolve(f.WorkingDir, overrides)

1600	func (f *CommandFactory) newAgentExecuteLoopCommand() *cobra.Command {
1601		cmd := &cobra.Command{
1602			Use:   "execute-loop",
1603			Short: "Drain the single-project execution-ready bead queue",
1604			Long: `execute-loop is the primary queue-driven execution surface. It scans the
1605	target project's execution-ready bead queue, claims the next ready bead,
1606	runs "ddx agent execute-bead" on it from the project root, records the
1607	structured result, and continues until no unattempted ready work remains.
1608	
1609	Reach for execute-loop by default. Use "ddx agent execute-bead" directly
1610	only as the primitive for debugging or re-running one specific bead.
1611	
1612	Planning and document-only beads are valid execution targets — any bead
1613	with unmet acceptance criteria and no blocking deps is eligible.
1614	
1615	Close semantics (per execute-bead result status):
1616	  success                      — close bead with session + commit evidence
1617	  already_satisfied            — close bead (after repeated no_changes)
1618	  no_changes                   — unclaim; may cooldown or close after retries
1619	  land_conflict                — unclaim; result preserved under refs/ddx/iterations/
1620	  post_run_check_failed        — unclaim; result preserved
1621	  execution_failed             — unclaim
1622	  structural_validation_failed — unclaim
1623	
1624	Only success (and already_satisfied) closes the bead. Every other status
1625	leaves the bead open and unclaimed so a later attempt can try again. Each
1626	attempt is appended to the bead as an execute-bead event (status, detail,
1627	base_rev, result_rev, preserve_ref, retry_after), and the underlying agent
1628	session log is recorded under the execute-bead agent-log path.
1629	
1630	By default execute-loop submits to the running ddx server as a background
1631	worker and returns immediately. Use --local to run inline in the current
1632	process.
1633	
1634	Project targeting (multi-project servers):
1635	  --project <path>    target a specific project root (absolute path or name)
1636	  DDX_PROJECT_ROOT    env var fallback; used when --project is not set
1637	  (default)           the git root of the current working directory
1638	
1639	When submitting to a multi-project server you must ensure the target project
1640	is registered with the server (run "ddx server" from that directory, or use
1641	"ddx server projects register"). The server rejects unrecognised project paths.
1642	`,
1643			Example: `  # Drain the current execution-ready queue once and exit (normal surface)
1644	  ddx agent execute-loop
1645	
1646	  # Pick one ready bead, execute it, and stop
1647	  ddx agent execute-loop --profile default --once
1648	
1649	  # Run continuously as a bounded queue worker
1650	  ddx agent execute-loop --poll-interval 30s
1651	
1652	  # Force a specific harness/model for a debugging pass
1653	  ddx agent execute-loop --once --harness codex
1654	  ddx agent execute-loop --once --harness agent --model minimax/minimax-m2.7
1655	
1656	  # Run inline in the current process (not recommended for long runs)
1657	  ddx agent execute-loop --local --once`,
1658			Args: cobra.NoArgs,
1659			RunE: f.runAgentExecuteLoop,
1660		}
1661		cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Target project root path or name (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT")
1662		cmd.Flags().String("from", "", "Base git revision to start from (default: HEAD)")
1663		cmd.Flags().String("harness", "", "Agent harness to use")
1664		cmd.Flags().String("model", "", "Model override")
1665		cmd.Flags().String("profile", agent.DefaultRoutingProfile, "Routing profile: default, cheap, fast, or smart")
1666		cmd.Flags().String("provider", "", "Provider name (e.g. vidar, openrouter); selects a named provider from config")
1667		cmd.Flags().String("model-ref", "", "Model catalog reference (e.g. code-medium); resolved via the model catalog")
1668		cmd.Flags().String("effort", "", "Effort level")
1669		cmd.Flags().Bool("once", false, "Process at most one ready bead")
1670		cmd.Flags().Duration("poll-interval", 0, "Poll interval for continuous scanning; zero drains current ready work and exits")
1671		cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "Output loop result as JSON")
1672		cmd.Flags().Bool("local", false, "Run inline in current process instead of server worker (default: submit to server)")
1673		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review", false, "Skip post-merge review (e.g. for doc-only beads or tight iteration loops)")
1674		cmd.Flags().String("review-harness", "", "Harness to use for the post-merge reviewer (default: same as implementation harness)")
1675		cmd.Flags().String("review-model", "", "Model override for the post-merge reviewer (default: smart tier)")
1676		cmd.Flags().Float64("max-cost", escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD, "Stop the loop when accumulated billed cost exceeds USD; 0 = unlimited; subscription and local providers do not count")
1677		cmd.Flags().Duration("request-timeout", 0, "Per-request provider wall-clock timeout (e.g. 60m, 2h); overrides project config and model-class defaults. Use when a thinking model needs more than the default 15 min.")
1678		cmd.Flags().Int("min-power", 0, "Minimum model power required (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
1679		cmd.Flags().Int("max-power", 0, "Maximum model power allowed (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
1680		return cmd
1681	}

1683	func (f *CommandFactory) runAgentExecuteLoop(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
1684		projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
1685		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
1686		fromRev, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("from")
1687		harness, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("harness")
1688		model, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model")
1689		profile, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("profile")
1690		provider, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("provider")
1691		modelRef, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model-ref")
1692		effort, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("effort")
1693		once, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("once")
1694		pollInterval, _ := cmd.Flags().GetDuration("poll-interval")
1695		asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
1696		local, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("local")
1697		noReview, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-review")
1698		reviewHarness, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("review-harness")
1699		reviewModel, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("review-model")
1700		maxCostUSD, _ := cmd.Flags().GetFloat64("max-cost")
1701		requestTimeout, _ := cmd.Flags().GetDuration("request-timeout")
1702		minPower, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("min-power")
1703		maxPower, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("max-power")
1704	
1705		// If --local, run inline; otherwise submit to running ddx server
1706		if !local {
1707			return f.executeLoopWithServer(cmd, projectRoot, harness, model, profile, provider, modelRef, effort, once, pollInterval, asJSON, noReview, reviewHarness, reviewModel)
1708		}
1709	
1710		// Pre-flight: validate harness availability and model compatibility
1711		// before claiming any beads. This surfaces errors like "claude binary
1712		// not found" or "vidar is an agent preset, not a claude model" before
1713		// any bead status changes hands.
1714		if err := agent.ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService(cmd.Context(), f.WorkingDir, harness, model, provider, modelRef); err != nil {
1715			return fmt.Errorf("execute-loop: %w", err)
1716		}
1717	
1718		store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx"))
1719	
1720		// Structured progress sink for this loop run. Events emitted at
1721		// loop.start, bead.claimed, bead.result, and loop.end land here so
1722		// log aggregators (FormatSessionLogLines, `ddx server workers log`)
1723		// can parse the same JSONL envelope used by harness session logs.
1724		loopSessionID := fmt.Sprintf("agent-loop-%d", time.Now().UnixNano())
1725		loopLogDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, agent.DefaultLogDir)
1726		_ = os.MkdirAll(loopLogDir, 0o755)
1727		loopLogPath := filepath.Join(loopLogDir, loopSessionID+".jsonl")
1728		var loopSink io.Writer
1729		if f, err := os.OpenFile(loopLogPath, os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY|os.O_APPEND, 0o644); err == nil {
1730			loopSink = f
1731			defer f.Close() //nolint:errcheck
1732		}
1733	
1734		// Start session log tailer so the user sees progress during agent execution
1735		tailCtx, tailCancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
1736		go agent.TailSessionLogs(tailCtx, projectRoot, cmd.OutOrStdout())
1737	
1738		// Instantiate a process-local LandCoordinator so --local uses the same
1739		// single-writer land path as the server worker. Stopped on function exit.
1740		localCoord := serverpkg.NewLocalLandCoordinator(projectRoot, agent.RealLandingGitOps{})
1741		defer localCoord.Stop()
1742	
1743		// Build post-merge reviewer unless --no-review is set.
1744		// Reviewer is on-by-default: runs at smart tier after every successful merge.
1745		var reviewer agent.BeadReviewer
1746		if !noReview {
1747			reviewer = &agent.DefaultBeadReviewer{
1748				ProjectRoot: projectRoot,
1749				BeadStore:   bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")),
1750				Harness:     reviewHarness,
1751				Model:       reviewModel,
1752			}
1753		}
1754	
1755		profile = agent.NormalizeRoutingProfile(profile)
1756	
1757		// Cost-cap state shared across attempts for this loop run.
1758		// paths. Accumulated billed spend (excluding local and subscription
1759		// providers) above --max-cost trips the cap and halts further bead
1760		// claiming. See escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD / CountsTowardCostCap.
1761		costCap := escalation.NewCostCapTracker(maxCostUSD, func(harnessName string) bool {
1762			// Resolve the harness's billing class via the service. Treat any

1764			// early than silently overshoot).
1765			svc, svcErr := agent.NewServiceFromWorkDir(f.WorkingDir)
1766			if svcErr != nil {
1767				return true
1768			}
1769			infos, err := svc.ListHarnesses(context.Background())
1770			if err != nil {

1772			}
1773			for _, h := range infos {

1775					return escalation.CountsTowardCostCap(h.IsLocal, h.IsSubscription, h.CostClass)

1790				Detail: detail,

18	// ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime carries the non-serializable plumbing and
19	// per-invocation runtime intent for an execute-bead loop run. Durable knobs
20	// (assignee, retry caps, harness/model, tier bounds, etc.) live on
21	// config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via Run's rcfg argument.
22	type ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime struct {
23		Log          io.Writer
24		EventSink    io.Writer
25		ProgressCh   chan<- ProgressEvent
26		PreClaimHook func(ctx context.Context) error
27		// RoutePreflight, when non-nil, is invoked between nextCandidate and
28		// Claim. It is expected to call upstream ResolveRoute against the
29		// loop's resolved (harness, model) and return whatever typed routing
30		// error the upstream surfaced — notably agent.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible
31		// when the harness allow-list rejects the model. Any non-nil error
32		// causes the loop to exit immediately without claiming the bead, with
33		// a worker-level execution_failed record naming the rejected pair.
34		// DDx does NOT duplicate the upstream allow-list; this gate only
35		// consumes the typed-incompatibility surface.
36		RoutePreflight func(ctx context.Context, harness, model string) error
37		Once           bool
38		PollInterval   time.Duration
39		NoReview       bool
40		LabelFilter    string
41		SessionID      string
42		WorkerID       string
43		ProjectRoot    string
44	}
45	
46	// DefaultReviewMaxRetries is the number of reviewer attempts allowed per
47	// committed result_rev before the loop emits a terminal
48	// `review-manual-required` event and stops re-executing primary. FEAT-022 §14.
49	const DefaultReviewMaxRetries = 3
50	
51	// MaxLoopCooldown is the absolute upper bound the execute-loop will set for
52	// any execute-loop-retry-after value. Year-scale parks effectively mean
53	// "never retry" and that should be a deliberate operator choice via
54	// `ddx bead update --set execute-loop-retry-after=...`, not an automatic
55	// loop decision. Beyond this cap, the loop refuses to lengthen the cooldown
56	// further; an operator extending it manually still works.
57	const MaxLoopCooldown = 24 * time.Hour
58	
59	// LandConflictCooldown is the short cooldown applied when all conflict-recovery
60	// paths fail (3-way ort auto-merge + optional focused-resolve agent) and the
61	// bead is parked as land_conflict_unresolvable. Much shorter than
62	// MaxLoopCooldown because land conflicts often resolve quickly as sibling beads
63	// advance the base branch.
64	const LandConflictCooldown = 15 * time.Minute
65	
66	// StoreErrorCooldown is the short cooldown applied when a Store.* operation in
67	// the outcome-handling block returns a transient error. It prevents an
68	// immediate re-queue of the affected bead while allowing the loop to continue
69	// to the next candidate. Store errors during outcome handling are non-fatal:
70	// they are logged and emitted as loop-error events but do not terminate the
71	// worker.
72	const StoreErrorCooldown = 5 * time.Minute
73	
74	// handleOutcomeStoreError is called when a Store.* operation in the
75	// post-Execute outcome block returns a non-nil error. It logs the error,
76	// appends a kind:loop-error event to the bead (best-effort), sets a short
77	// cooldown so the bead is not immediately re-queued, increments
78	// result.Failures, and returns true — signalling the caller to continue to
79	// the next loop iteration rather than returning.
80	//
81	// If ctx is already cancelled, it returns false so the caller can propagate
82	// the cancellation immediately.
83	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) handleOutcomeStoreError(ctx context.Context, beadID, operation string, storeErr error, assignee string, result *ExecuteBeadLoopResult, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime, now func() time.Time) bool {
84		if ctx.Err() != nil {
85			return false
86		}
87		if runtime.Log != nil {
88			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "outcome store error (%s %s): %v (continuing)\n", operation, beadID, storeErr)
89		}
90		_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
91			Kind:      "loop-error",
92			Summary:   operation + " failed",
93			Body:      storeErr.Error(),
94			Actor:     assignee,
95			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
96			CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
97		})
98		cooldownUntil := now().UTC().Add(StoreErrorCooldown)
99		_ = w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(beadID, cooldownUntil, "loop-error", operation+": "+storeErr.Error())
100		result.Failures++
101		result.LastFailureStatus = "loop-error"
102		return true
103	}
104	
105	// capLoopCooldown clamps a loop-set cooldown duration to MaxLoopCooldown.
106	// The loop uses this for every SetExecutionCooldown call so no automatic
107	// path can silently park a bead for a year.
108	func capLoopCooldown(d time.Duration) time.Duration {
109		if d <= 0 {
110			return 0
111		}
112		if d > MaxLoopCooldown {
113			return MaxLoopCooldown
114		}
115		return d
116	}
117	
118	type ExecuteBeadReport struct {
119		BeadID      string `json:"bead_id"`
120		AttemptID   string `json:"attempt_id,omitempty"`
121		WorkerID    string `json:"worker_id,omitempty"`
122		Harness     string `json:"harness,omitempty"`
123		Provider    string `json:"provider,omitempty"`
124		Model       string `json:"model,omitempty"`
125		Status      string `json:"status"`
126		Detail      string `json:"detail,omitempty"`
127		SessionID   string `json:"session_id,omitempty"`
128		BaseRev     string `json:"base_rev,omitempty"`
129		ResultRev   string `json:"result_rev,omitempty"`
130		PreserveRef string `json:"preserve_ref,omitempty"`
131		RetryAfter  string `json:"retry_after,omitempty"`
132		// NoChangesRationale carries the agent's explanation when status == no_changes.
133		NoChangesRationale string `json:"no_changes_rationale,omitempty"`
134		// ReviewVerdict is the post-merge review verdict (APPROVE, REQUEST_CHANGES,

8	// newWorkCommand creates the top-level "ddx work" command as a UX alias for
9	// "ddx agent execute-loop". All flags and behavior are identical.
10	func (f *CommandFactory) newWorkCommand() *cobra.Command {
11		inner := f.newAgentExecuteLoopCommand()
12	
13		cmd := &cobra.Command{
14			Use:   "work",
15			Short: "Work the bead execution queue",
16			Long: `work is the primary operator-facing surface for draining the bead
17	execution queue. It is an alias for "ddx agent execute-loop" — all flags
18	and behavior are identical.
19	
20	` + inner.Long,
21			Example: `  # Drain the current execution-ready queue once and exit
22	  ddx work
23	
24	  # Pick one ready bead, execute it, and stop
25	  ddx work --profile default --once
26	
27	  # Run continuously as a bounded queue worker
28	  ddx work --poll-interval 30s
29	
30	  # Force a specific harness/model for a debugging pass
31	  ddx work --once --harness agent --model minimax/minimax-m2.7
32	
33	  # Run inline in the current process
34	  ddx work --local --once`,
35			Args: inner.Args,
36			RunE: inner.RunE,
37		}
38	
39		// Clone all flags from the execute-loop command
40		inner.Flags().VisitAll(func(flag *pflag.Flag) {
41			cmd.Flags().AddFlag(flag)
42		})
43	
44		return cmd
45	}

23	// ExecuteBeadResult captures the outcome of an execute-bead worker run.
24	// The worker populates the task-level fields (BeadID through UsageFile).
25	// The parent orchestrator (LandBeadResult) populates the landing fields
26	// (Outcome, Status, Detail, Reason, PreserveRef, GateResults, RequiredExecSummary,
27	// ChecksFile) via ApplyLandingToResult before the result is written to disk or
28	// returned to a caller.

30		BeadID    string `json:"bead_id"`

32		WorkerID  string `json:"worker_id,omitempty"`
33		BaseRev   string `json:"base_rev"`
34		ResultRev string `json:"result_rev,omitempty"`
35	
36		// Outcome and Status are initially set by the worker to task-level values
37		// (task_succeeded / task_failed / task_no_changes), then overwritten by
38		// ApplyLandingToResult with the landing decision (merged / preserved /
39		// no-changes) so callers see a unified record.
40		Outcome string `json:"outcome"`
41		Status  string `json:"status,omitempty"`
42		Detail  string `json:"detail,omitempty"`
43	
44		// Landing fields — populated by ApplyLandingToResult, not by ExecuteBead.
45		Reason              string            `json:"reason,omitempty"`
46		PreserveRef         string            `json:"preserve_ref,omitempty"`
47		GateResults         []GateCheckResult `json:"gate_results,omitempty"`
48		RequiredExecSummary string            `json:"required_exec_summary,omitempty"`
49		ChecksFile          string            `json:"checks_file,omitempty"`
50		// Ratchet fields — populated by ApplyLandingToResult when declarative
51		// ratchet thresholds were evaluated during landing. HELIX and other
52		// consumers use these to distinguish ratchet-preserved attempts from
53		// generic execution failures.
54		RatchetEvidence []RatchetEvidence `json:"ratchet_evidence,omitempty"`
55		RatchetSummary  string            `json:"ratchet_summary,omitempty"`
56	
57		// NoChangesRationale is populated when outcome == task_no_changes and the
58		// agent wrote a rationale file to the execution bundle dir inside the
59		// worktree. It carries the agent's explanation of why no commits were made.
60		NoChangesRationale string `json:"no_changes_rationale,omitempty"`

117	// GitOps abstracts the git operations required by the worker.
118	// Merge is intentionally excluded — that belongs to the parent-side
119	// orchestrator (OrchestratorGitOps). UpdateRef/DeleteRef are exposed here
120	// so landing-side helpers (e.g. BuildLandingGateContext) can pin a
121	// transient ref while running gate evaluation against an ephemeral
122	// worktree.
123	type GitOps interface {
124		HeadRev(dir string) (string, error)
125		ResolveRev(dir, rev string) (string, error)
126		WorktreeAdd(dir, wtPath, rev string) error
127		WorktreeRemove(dir, wtPath string) error
128		WorktreeList(dir string) ([]string, error)
129		WorktreePrune(dir string) error
130		IsDirty(dir string) (bool, error)
131		// SynthesizeCommit stages real file changes (excluding harness noise paths) and
132		// commits them using msg as the commit message. Returns (true, nil) when a
133		// commit was made, (false, nil) when there was nothing real to commit (all
134		// dirty files were noise), and (false, err) on failure.
135		SynthesizeCommit(dir, msg string) (bool, error)
136		// UpdateRef updates ref in dir to sha. Used by landing helpers to pin a
137		// commit so a transient worktree can check it out without racing with
138		// other work that might prune it.
139		UpdateRef(dir, ref, sha string) error
140		// DeleteRef removes ref from dir. Used to unpin a transient ref after
141		// the consumer (e.g. an ephemeral worktree) is done with it.
142		DeleteRef(dir, ref string) error
143	}

217	// Constants for worktree and artifact paths.

219		ExecuteBeadWtDir       = ".ddx" // legacy; kept for compatibility
220		ExecuteBeadWtPrefix    = ".execute-bead-wt-"
221		ExecuteBeadArtifactDir = ".ddx/executions"
222	
223		// ExecuteBeadTmpSubdir is the subdirectory under $TMPDIR in which
224		// execute-bead creates its isolated worktrees. Keeping them outside
225		// the project tree prevents child processes (tests, hooks) running
226		// inside the worktree from mutating the parent repository's
227		// .git/config via inherited GIT_DIR.
228		ExecuteBeadTmpSubdir = "ddx-exec-wt"

231	// executeBeadWorktreePath returns the absolute path where an execute-bead
232	// isolated worktree for (beadID, attemptID) should live.
233	func executeBeadWorktreePath(beadID, attemptID string) string {
234		base := os.Getenv("DDX_EXEC_WT_DIR")
235		if base == "" {
236			base = filepath.Join(os.TempDir(), ExecuteBeadTmpSubdir)
237		}
238		return filepath.Join(base, ExecuteBeadWtPrefix+beadID+"-"+attemptID)
239	}

59	// run path. Durable knobs (Harness, Model, Provider, ModelRef, Effort,
60	// Permissions, Timeout, WallClock, SessionLogDir) are stripped — they
61	// live on config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via RunWithConfig's
62	// rcfg argument. Only non-serializable plumbing and per-invocation
63	// runtime intent remain.

80		// without re-resolving the full ResolvedConfig. SD-024 step B22d-d:
81		// execute-bead pins permissions=unrestricted; the post-merge reviewer
82		// pins harness/model to the reviewer-tier selection.

## Agent Execution Code Structure - Comprehensive Report

Based on my thorough exploration of the DDx repository, here are the key findings about agent execution:

### 1. **AgentPassthrough & AgentConstraints Types**

These types **do not exist yet in the current codebase**. However, the equivalent functionality is implemented through:
- **MinPower/MaxPower passthrough**: Defined in `cli/internal/config/CLIOverrides` struct (lines 35-36 of `/cli/internal/config/resolved.go`)
- These are passed to `agentlib.ServiceExecuteRequest` as `.MinPower()` and `.MaxPower()` accessor methods on `ResolvedConfig`
- The upstream `agentlib.ServiceExecuteRequest` struct (from external `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent` package) contains the actual `MinPower` and `MaxPower` fields

### 2. **ServiceExecuteRequest Structure**

Located in external agentlib package, but constructed in:
- **File**: `/cli/internal/agent/service_run.go` (lines 159-175)
- **Key fields populated**:
  - `Prompt`, `Model`, `Provider`, `Harness`, `ModelRef`
  - `Reasoning` (from effort), `Permissions`
  - `WorkDir`, `Timeout`, `IdleTimeout`, `ProviderTimeout`
  - `SessionLogDir`, `Metadata` (correlation)
  - **`MinPower`, `MaxPower`** (lines 173-174) - passed from `ResolvedConfig`

### 3. **CLI Flag Handling (--harness, --provider, --model)**

**execute-bead command** (`/cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go`):
- Lines 90-99: Flag definitions for `--harness`, `--model`, `--provider`, `--model-ref`, `--effort`, `--min-power`, `--max-power`
- Lines 112-122: Flag retrieval in `runAgentExecuteBead`
- Lines 126-138: Packaged into `config.CLIOverrides` struct

**execute-loop command** (`/cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go` lines 1600-1681):
- Lines 1663-1679: Flag definitions (same as execute-bead)
- Lines 1687-1703: Flag retrieval in `runAgentExecuteLoop`

**work command** (`/cli/cmd/work.go`):
- Alias for `execute-loop` with identical flags

### 4. **Execute-Loop / Work Command Flow**

**Primary entry point**: `CommandFactory.runAgentExecuteLoop` (`/cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go` lines 1683+)

**Flow**:
1. **Flag parsing**: Extract all CLI flags including harness, model, provider, min/max-power
2. **Server submission or local execution**: Check `--local` flag (line 1706)
3. **Pre-flight validation**: `agent.ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService` (line 1714) - validates harness/model routing before claiming beads
4. **Bead store initialization**: `bead.NewStore` (line 1718)
5. **Loop session setup**: Creates JSONL event sink and progress channels (lines 1724-1732)
6. **Cost cap tracking**: Instantiates `escalation.NewCostCapTracker` (line 1761)
7. **Per-bead execution**: Via `singleTierAttempt` callback (line 1797+)
8. **Outcome handling**: Records results via `Store.AppendEvent` and `Store.SetExecutionCooldown`

**Core loop structure** (`/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`):
- `ExecuteBeadWorker.Run()` - main loop implementation
- Implements `ExecuteBeadLoopStore` interface for bead state operations

### 5. **Retry / Cooldown / MinPower/MaxPower Logic**

**Location**: `/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`

**Retry/Cooldown Constants**:
- `MaxLoopCooldown = 24 * time.Hour` (line 57) - absolute upper bound for retry delays
- `LandConflictCooldown = 15 * time.Minute` (line 64) - short cooldown for merge conflicts
- `StoreErrorCooldown = 5 * time.Minute` (line 72) - when bead store operations fail
- `DefaultReviewMaxRetries = 3` (line 49) - reviewer attempt cap

**Cooldown Functions**:
- `capLoopCooldown()` (lines 105-116) - clamps any cooldown to MaxLoopCooldown
- `SetExecutionCooldown()` (line 206, interface definition) - parks bead until specified time

**MinPower/MaxPower Flow**:
- Extracted from CLI flags in execute-loop
- Passed to `config.CLIOverrides` (lines 35-36 of `resolved.go`)
- Resolved via `ResolvedConfig.MinPower()` / `ResolvedConfig.MaxPower()` accessors
- Passed to `agentlib.ServiceExecuteRequest` in `executeOnService()` (lines 173-174 of `service_run.go`)
- Service handles routing constraints; actual power selected returned as `actualPower` (line 186 of `service_run.go`)

**Retry Application** (in execute_bead_loop.go):
- Line 826: `SetExecutionCooldown` for land conflicts with `LandConflictCooldown`
- Line 893: `SetExecutionCooldown` for no-progress with `noProgressCooldown` (default 6 hours)
- Line 99: Store error handling applies `StoreErrorCooldown`

### 6. **Run Evidence Writing**

**Evidence directory structure**:
- Base: `.ddx/executions/<attemptID>/`
- Contains: `manifest.json`, `prompt.md`, `result.json`, `usage.json`, `checks.json`

**Key Functions**:
- `writeArtifactJSON()` (`/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go` lines 1459-1465) - writes JSON artifacts
- `ExecuteBeadResult` struct (lines 29-91 of `execute_bead.go`) - main result payload

**Evidence Writing Locations** in `execute_bead.go`:
- Line 659: Preliminary result.json write (before commit)
- Line 770: Final result.json after SynthesizeCommit
- Line 789: usage.json write (token/cost)
- Line 836: Fallback preliminary result (on execution failure)
- Line 935: Final result.json after all processing
- Line 1094: Manifest.json creation

**Event Appending** (bead-level evidence):
- `Store.AppendEvent()` called throughout loop (execute_bead_loop.go lines 90, 672, 687, 703, 711, 738, 769)
- Records kind-specific events: `execute-bead`, `review-manual-required`, `loop-error`
- Each event captures: summary, body, actor, source, created_at

**Loop Event Recording** (execute_bead_loop.go line 1724+):
- Session ID: `agent-loop-{UnixNano}`
- Log path: `.ddx/agent/<sessionID>.jsonl`
- Structured JSONL events emitted for loop lifecycle: start, bead.claimed, bead.result, loop.end

### 7. **Key Files Summary**

| Component | File Path | Key Types/Functions |
|-----------|-----------|-------------------|
| CLI Commands | `/cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go` | `newAgentExecuteLoopCommand()`, `runAgentExecuteLoop()` |
| Execute-Bead | `/cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go` | `runAgentExecuteBead()`, `writeExecuteBeadResult()` |
| Config Overrides | `/cli/internal/config/resolved.go` | `CLIOverrides`, `ResolvedConfig` |
| Service Integration | `/cli/internal/agent/service_run.go` | `RunWithConfigViaService()`, `executeOnService()` |
| Execute-Bead Worker | `/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go` | `ExecuteBeadResult`, `ExecuteBeadRuntime`, `writeArtifactJSON()` |
| Loop Implementation | `/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go` | `ExecuteBeadWorker`, `ExecuteBeadReport`, cooldown logic |
| Work Command | `/cli/cmd/work.go` | UX alias wrapper |
| Status Classification | `/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go` | `FailureMode*` constants, `ClassifyFailureMode()` |

### 8. **Notes on Design**

- **AgentPassthrough/AgentConstraints don't exist yet** - MinPower/MaxPower are simple int fields in `CLIOverrides` and `ResolvedConfig`, not complex constraint types
- **No dedicated constraint validation struct** - constraints are passed through as scalar values to the upstream agent service
- **Evidence is per-attempt, not per-bead** - each attempt gets its own timestamped `.ddx/executions/<attemptID>/` directory
- **Retry logic is cooldown-based** - not retry-count based; beads are parked until specified time, allowing concurrent sibling beads to advance

100		r.contextBudget = overrides.ContextBudget
101		if r.contextBudget == "" && c != nil {
102			r.contextBudget = c.EvidenceCaps.ResolveContextBudget()
103		}
104	
105		if agent != nil {
106			r.sessionLogDir = agent.SessionLogDir
107			if agent.ReasoningLevels != nil {
108				r.reasoningLevels = make(map[string][]string, len(agent.ReasoningLevels))
109				for k, v := range agent.ReasoningLevels {
110					r.reasoningLevels[k] = append([]string(nil), v...)
111				}
112			}
113		}
114		if c != nil && c.Executions != nil && c.Executions.Mirror != nil {
115			r.mirrorConfig = c.Executions.Mirror.Clone()
116		}
117	
118		return r
119	}
120	
121	// LoadAndResolve is the canonical production entry point that produces
122	// a sealed ResolvedConfig. It loads the project's .ddx/config.yaml from
123	// projectRoot, layers overrides on top, and returns the immutable
124	// ResolvedConfig.
125	//
126	// On load error, LoadAndResolve still returns a usable, sealed
127	// ResolvedConfig populated from package defaults plus the supplied
128	// overrides, alongside the underlying error. Callers decide whether to
129	// surface the error or proceed with the defaults-backed config.
130	//
131	// If projectRoot is empty, the process working directory is used.
132	func LoadAndResolve(projectRoot string, overrides CLIOverrides) (ResolvedConfig, error) {
133		cfg, err := LoadWithWorkingDir(projectRoot)
134		if err != nil {
135			return DefaultNewConfig().Resolve(overrides), err
136		}
137		return cfg.Resolve(overrides), nil
138	}
139	
140	// ResolvedConfig is the loop/runner/reviewer's view of merged project
141	// config plus per-invocation overrides. It is constructed only by
142	// (*Config).Resolve / config.LoadAndResolve and is safe to share across
143	// goroutines (no method mutates it).
144	//
145	// Sealed-construction: every public accessor calls requireSealed on
146	// entry. A zero-value ResolvedConfig{} or `var r ResolvedConfig` is a
147	// valid Go expression but fails loudly on first read with a panic
148	// naming LoadAndResolve. See SD-024 / TD-024.
149	type ResolvedConfig struct {
150		sealed bool
151	
152		assignee                string
153		reviewMaxRetries        int
154		noProgressCooldown      time.Duration
155		maxNoChangesBeforeClose int
156		heartbeatInterval       time.Duration
157		harness                 string
158		model                   string
159		provider                string
160		modelRef                string
161		profile                 string
162		minTier                 string
163		maxTier                 string
164		effort                  string
165		minPower                int
166		maxPower                int
167		permissions             string
168		timeout                 time.Duration
169		wallClock               time.Duration
170		contextBudget           string
171		evidenceCaps            evidence.Caps
172		sessionLogDir           string
173		mirrorConfig            *ExecutionsMirrorConfig
174		reasoningLevels         map[string][]string
175		providerRequestTimeout  time.Duration
176	}
177	
178	// requireSealed panics if r was not produced by Resolve / LoadAndResolve.
179	// Called as the first statement of every public accessor.
180	func (r ResolvedConfig) requireSealed() {
181		if !r.sealed {
182			panic("config: ResolvedConfig used without going through " +
183				"(*Config).Resolve or config.LoadAndResolve. " +
184				"Production callers must obtain a ResolvedConfig from " +
185				"LoadAndResolve; tests must use NewTestConfigFor*.")
186		}
187	}

317	func cloneStringSliceMap(m map[string][]string) map[string][]string {
318		if m == nil {
319			return nil
320		}
321		out := make(map[string][]string, len(m))
322		for k, v := range m {
323			out[k] = append([]string(nil), v...)

1	package agent
2	
3	import "strings"
4	
5	// PushFailedReasonPrefix is the canonical Reason prefix written by the
6	// landing layer when `git push` rejects the new tip after a local merge or
7	// fast-forward succeeded. ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus matches on this prefix
8	// to map a merged-locally-but-not-pushed outcome to push_failed instead of
9	// success, so the bead stays open.
10	const PushFailedReasonPrefix = "landed locally; push failed:"
11	
12	// PushConflictReasonPrefix marks a more specific failure mode than
13	// PushFailedReasonPrefix: the local push was rejected because the remote
14	// advanced, the loop attempted an automatic pull/merge to recover, and the
15	// merge produced a conflict the loop cannot resolve. The bead is parked for
16	// human review with a short cooldown rather than a year-long park.
17	const PushConflictReasonPrefix = "landed locally; push conflict:"
18	
19	// FailureMode constants classify *why* an execution did not land cleanly.
20	// They are orthogonal to Outcome/Status: a single failed attempt carries one
21	// outcome (e.g. preserved) and one failure mode (e.g. merge_conflict) so
22	// measurement surfaces can aggregate failures by cause.
23	const (
24		FailureModeContextOverflow     = "context_overflow"
25		FailureModeMergeConflict       = "merge_conflict"
26		FailureModeTestFailure         = "test_failure"
27		FailureModeBuildFailure        = "build_failure"
28		FailureModeTimeout             = "timeout"
29		FailureModeAuthError           = "auth_error"
30		FailureModeNoChanges           = "no_changes"
31		FailureModeNoEvidenceProduced  = "no_evidence_produced"
32		FailureModeRatchetMiss         = "ratchet_miss"
33		FailureModeNoViableProvider    = "no_viable_provider"
34		FailureModeHarnessNotInstalled = "harness_not_installed"
35		FailureModeUnknown             = "unknown"
36	)
37	
38	// ClassifyFailureMode derives a failure_mode for a worker-level result from
39	// its outcome, exit code, and error message. Returns "" when the outcome is
40	// task_succeeded (success carries no failure mode). task_no_changes always
41	// maps to FailureModeNoChanges regardless of error text.
42	//
43	// For task_failed (or non-zero exit code), the error message is scanned for
44	// well-known substrings. The order matters: context/auth/timeout patterns
45	// are checked before generic test/build patterns so a "test timed out"
46	// classifies as timeout rather than test_failure. Anything unrecognised
47	// maps to FailureModeUnknown so aggregates never hide a missed pattern.
48	func ClassifyFailureMode(outcome string, exitCode int, errMsg string) string {
49		switch outcome {
50		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded:
51			if exitCode == 0 {
52				return ""
53			}
54			// Degenerate: success outcome with non-zero exit. Treat as unknown
55			// failure rather than success so the aggregate flags the anomaly.
56		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
57			return FailureModeNoChanges
58		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence:
59			return FailureModeNoEvidenceProduced
60		}
61	
62		lower := strings.ToLower(errMsg)
63		switch {
64		case containsAny(lower,
65			"executable file not found in $path",
66			"executable file not found",
67			"no such file or directory") &&
68			containsAny(lower, "exec:", "harness", "claude", "agent", "codex", "gemini"):
69			return FailureModeHarnessNotInstalled
70		case containsAny(lower,
71			"no viable harness",
72			"no harness configured",
73			"failed to initialize routing service",
74			"no viable provider"):
75			return FailureModeNoViableProvider
76		case containsAny(lower,
77			"context length",
78			"context_length_exceeded",
79			"context window",
80			"maximum context",
81			"prompt is too long",
82			"prompt too long",
83			"token limit",
84			"too many tokens",
85			"exceeds the context"):
86			return FailureModeContextOverflow
87		case containsAny(lower,
88			"unauthorized",
89			"401 unauthorized",
90			"invalid api key",
91			"invalid_api_key",
92			"authentication failed",
93			"authentication_error",
94			"no api key",
95			"missing api key",
96			"quota exceeded",
97			"insufficient quota",
98			"insufficient_quota",
99			"rate limit",
100			"rate_limit",
101			"429"):
102			return FailureModeAuthError
103		case containsAny(lower,
104			"timeout",
105			"timed out",
106			"context deadline exceeded",
107			"deadline exceeded",
108			"signal: killed"):
109			return FailureModeTimeout
110		case containsAny(lower,
111			"merge conflict",
112			"ff-merge not possible",
113			"merge failed",
114			"automatic merge failed"):
115			return FailureModeMergeConflict
116		case containsAny(lower,
117			"build failed",
118			"compilation failed",
119			"compile error",
120			"compilation error",
121			"cannot find package",
122			"undefined:",
123			"undefined reference",
124			"syntax error"):
125			return FailureModeBuildFailure
126		case containsAny(lower,
127			"test failed",
128			"tests failed",
129			"--- fail:",
130			"assertion failed",
131			"test failure"):
132			return FailureModeTestFailure
133		}
134		if exitCode != 0 {
135			return FailureModeUnknown
136		}
137		return ""
138	}
139	
140	// containsAny reports whether s contains any of the given substrings. s is
141	// assumed to already be lower-cased by the caller.
142	func containsAny(s string, needles ...string) bool {
143		for _, n := range needles {
144			if n == "" {
145				continue
146			}
147			if strings.Contains(s, n) {
148				return true
149			}
150		}
151		return false
152	}
153	
154	// classifyLandingFailureMode computes the final failure_mode for a result
155	// after the orchestrator has made its landing decision. It prefers
156	// landing-specific signals (merge conflict, gate failure) over the worker's
157	// classification and falls back to workerMode for worker-level failures
158	// (agent execution failed, context overflow, etc.). Returns "" when the
159	// bead merged cleanly.
160	func classifyLandingFailureMode(landingOutcome, landingReason string, gateResults []GateCheckResult, workerMode string) string {
161		switch landingOutcome {
162		case "merged":
163			return ""
164		case "no-changes", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
165			return FailureModeNoChanges
166		case "no-evidence", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence:
167			return FailureModeNoEvidenceProduced
168		case "error":
169			if workerMode != "" {
170				return workerMode
171			}
172			return FailureModeUnknown
173		case "preserved":
174			switch landingReason {
175			case "merge conflict", "merge failed", "ff-merge not possible":
176				return FailureModeMergeConflict
177			case "post-run checks failed":
178				return classifyGateFailure(gateResults)
179			case RatchetPreserveReason:
180				return FailureModeRatchetMiss
181			}
182			// Other preserved reasons (e.g. "agent execution failed",
183			// "--no-merge specified") defer to the worker's classification.
184			if workerMode != "" {
185				return workerMode
186			}
187			return FailureModeUnknown
188		}
189		if workerMode != "" {
190			return workerMode
191		}
192		return FailureModeUnknown
193	}
194	
195	// classifyGateFailure inspects failed gate results to distinguish
196	// build_failure from test_failure. Gate definitions that fail to compile
197	// before running (go build, tsc, cargo build) typically surface build
198	// diagnostics in stderr; test runners surface test failure markers. When
199	// ambiguous, defaults to test_failure since the post-run checks gate is
200	// test-oriented by convention.
201	func classifyGateFailure(gateResults []GateCheckResult) string {
202		for _, gr := range gateResults {
203			if gr.Status != "fail" {
204				continue
205			}
206			combined := strings.ToLower(gr.Stdout + "\n" + gr.Stderr)
207			if containsAny(combined,
208				"build failed",
209				"compilation failed",
210				"compile error",
211				"compilation error",
212				"cannot find package",
213				"undefined:",
214				"undefined reference",
215				"syntax error") {
216				return FailureModeBuildFailure
217			}
218		}
219		return FailureModeTestFailure
220	}
221	
222	// Worker-level outcome constants — set by ExecuteBead() on the result.
223	// The parent orchestrator (LandBeadResult + ApplyLandingToResult) then
224	// overwrites Outcome and Status with the landing decision.
225	const (
226		ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded  = "task_succeeded"
227		ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed     = "task_failed"
228		ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges  = "task_no_changes"
229		ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence = "task_no_evidence"
230	)
231	
232	// Status constants — used by the loop and other consumers of ExecuteBeadReport.
233	// These map to specific close/unclaim behaviors in ExecuteBeadWorker.
234	const (
235		ExecuteBeadStatusStructuralValidationFailed = "structural_validation_failed"
236		ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed            = "execution_failed"
237		ExecuteBeadStatusPostRunCheckFailed         = "post_run_check_failed"
238		ExecuteBeadStatusRatchetFailed              = "ratchet_failed"
239		ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflict               = "land_conflict"
240		ExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed                 = "push_failed"
241		ExecuteBeadStatusPushConflict               = "push_conflict"
242		ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview       = "preserved_needs_review"
243		ExecuteBeadStatusNoEvidenceProduced         = "no_evidence_produced"
244		ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges                  = "no_changes"
245		ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied           = "already_satisfied"
246		ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess                    = "success"
247	
248		// ExecuteBeadStatusDeclinedNeedsDecomposition is a structured outcome
249		// signalling that the executor inspected the bead and concluded it cannot
250		// be delivered in a single pass — typically because the scope is
251		// epic-sized and must first be broken into sub-beads. It is distinct
252		// from no_changes (which means "I tried and there was nothing to change")
253		// and from execution_failed (which means the agent or harness errored).
254		//
255		// The loop treats this status as terminal-for-loop: it parks the bead
256		// with a long cooldown and records the recommended sub-beads as a
257		// structured `decomposition-recommendation` event. No further attempts
258		// happen until an operator clears the cooldown (e.g. via
259		// `ddx bead cooldown clear`) or splits the bead.
260		ExecuteBeadStatusDeclinedNeedsDecomposition = "declined_needs_decomposition"
261	
262		// Post-merge review outcomes. The bead was merged, then reviewed;
263		// the review returned a non-APPROVE verdict and the bead was reopened.
264		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewRequestChanges = "review_request_changes"
265		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewBlock          = "review_block"
266		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewMalfunction    = "review_malfunction"
267	
268		// ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictUnresolvable is set when execute-loop
269		// attempted 3-way ort auto-resolution and (if configured) a focused
270		// conflict-resolve agent run for a preserved iteration, and both failed.
271		// The bead is parked under LandConflictCooldown for operator review.
272		ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictUnresolvable = "land_conflict_unresolvable"
273	
274		// ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictNeedsHuman is set when the focused
275		// conflict-resolve agent escalated to BLOCKING — the conflict requires
276		// human judgment, not automated retry. Parked under the same short
277		// cooldown with a kind:land-conflict-needs-human event.
278		ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictNeedsHuman = "land_conflict_needs_human"
279	)
280	
281	// ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus maps a landing outcome to the supervisor-visible
282	// status contract. This is called by ApplyLandingToResult and by callers who
283	// build an ExecuteBeadReport from a landing result.
284	func ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(outcome string, exitCode int, reason string) string {
285		if exitCode != 0 {
286			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
287		}
288	
289		switch outcome {
290		case "merged":
291			// A merged outcome whose reason carries the push-failure marker
292			// means the local target branch advanced but `git push` rejected the
293			// new tip (large blob, branch protection, auth, divergence, etc.).
294			// Classify these as push_failed so the loop refuses to close the
295			// bead and operators are forced to investigate.
296			if strings.HasPrefix(reason, PushConflictReasonPrefix) {
297				return ExecuteBeadStatusPushConflict
298			}
299			if strings.HasPrefix(reason, PushFailedReasonPrefix) {
300				return ExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed
301			}
302			return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
303		case "no-changes", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
304			return ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges
305		case "no-evidence", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence:
306			return ExecuteBeadStatusNoEvidenceProduced
307		case "error":
308			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
309		case "preserved":
310			switch reason {
311			case "merge conflict", "merge failed", "ff-merge not possible":
312				return ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflict
313			case "post-run checks failed":
314				return ExecuteBeadStatusPostRunCheckFailed
315			case RatchetPreserveReason:
316				return ExecuteBeadStatusRatchetFailed
317			default:
318				if isPreservedNeedsReviewReason(reason) {
319					return ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview
320				}
321				// Preserved iterations may still be success when the caller
322				// explicitly requested no merge.
323				return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
324			}
325		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded:
326			return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
327		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed:
328			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
329		default:
330			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
331		}
332	}
333	
334	func isPreservedNeedsReviewReason(reason string) bool {
335		for _, prefix := range []string{
336			"large-deletion gate:",
337			"syntax sanity gate:",
338			"post-land gate failed:",
339		} {
340			if strings.HasPrefix(reason, prefix) {
341				return true
342			}
343		}
344		return false
345	}
346	
347	func ExecuteBeadStatusDetail(status, reason, errMsg string) string {
348		switch {
349		case reason != "" && errMsg != "" && reason != errMsg:
350			return reason + ": " + errMsg
351		case reason != "":
352			return reason
353		case errMsg != "":
354			return errMsg
355		case status != "":
356			return status
357		default:
358			return "unknown"
359		}
360	}

18		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"

23	// ExecuteBeadResult captures the outcome of an execute-bead worker run.
24	// The worker populates the task-level fields (BeadID through UsageFile).
25	// The parent orchestrator (LandBeadResult) populates the landing fields
26	// (Outcome, Status, Detail, Reason, PreserveRef, GateResults, RequiredExecSummary,
27	// ChecksFile) via ApplyLandingToResult before the result is written to disk or
28	// returned to a caller.
29	type ExecuteBeadResult struct {
30		BeadID    string `json:"bead_id"`
31		AttemptID string `json:"attempt_id,omitempty"`
32		WorkerID  string `json:"worker_id,omitempty"`
33		BaseRev   string `json:"base_rev"`
34		ResultRev string `json:"result_rev,omitempty"`
35	
36		// Outcome and Status are initially set by the worker to task-level values
37		// (task_succeeded / task_failed / task_no_changes), then overwritten by
38		// ApplyLandingToResult with the landing decision (merged / preserved /
39		// no-changes) so callers see a unified record.
40		Outcome string `json:"outcome"`
41		Status  string `json:"status,omitempty"`
42		Detail  string `json:"detail,omitempty"`
43	
44		// Landing fields — populated by ApplyLandingToResult, not by ExecuteBead.
45		Reason              string            `json:"reason,omitempty"`
46		PreserveRef         string            `json:"preserve_ref,omitempty"`
47		GateResults         []GateCheckResult `json:"gate_results,omitempty"`
48		RequiredExecSummary string            `json:"required_exec_summary,omitempty"`
49		ChecksFile          string            `json:"checks_file,omitempty"`
50		// Ratchet fields — populated by ApplyLandingToResult when declarative
51		// ratchet thresholds were evaluated during landing. HELIX and other
52		// consumers use these to distinguish ratchet-preserved attempts from
53		// generic execution failures.
54		RatchetEvidence []RatchetEvidence `json:"ratchet_evidence,omitempty"`
55		RatchetSummary  string            `json:"ratchet_summary,omitempty"`
56	
57		// NoChangesRationale is populated when outcome == task_no_changes and the
58		// agent wrote a rationale file to the execution bundle dir inside the
59		// worktree. It carries the agent's explanation of why no commits were made.
60		NoChangesRationale string `json:"no_changes_rationale,omitempty"`

231	// executeBeadWorktreePath returns the absolute path where an execute-bead
232	// isolated worktree for (beadID, attemptID) should live.
233	func executeBeadWorktreePath(beadID, attemptID string) string {
234		base := os.Getenv("DDX_EXEC_WT_DIR")
235		if base == "" {
236			base = filepath.Join(os.TempDir(), ExecuteBeadTmpSubdir)
237		}
238		return filepath.Join(base, ExecuteBeadWtPrefix+beadID+"-"+attemptID)

241	// RealGitOps implements GitOps via os/exec git commands.
242	type RealGitOps struct{}

244	func (r *RealGitOps) HeadRev(dir string) (string, error) {
245		return r.ResolveRev(dir, "HEAD")
246	}
247	
248	func (r *RealGitOps) ResolveRev(dir, rev string) (string, error) {
249		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "rev-parse", rev).Output()
250		if err != nil {
251			return "", fmt.Errorf("git rev-parse %s: %w", rev, err)
252		}
253		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), nil
254	}
255	
256	func (r *RealGitOps) WorktreeAdd(dir, wtPath, rev string) error {
257		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "worktree", "add", "--detach", wtPath, rev).CombinedOutput()
258		if err != nil {
259			return fmt.Errorf("git worktree add: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
260		}
261		return nil
262	}
263	
264	func (r *RealGitOps) WorktreeRemove(dir, wtPath string) error {
265		_ = internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "worktree", "remove", "--force", wtPath).Run()
266		return nil
267	}
268	
269	func (r *RealGitOps) WorktreeList(dir string) ([]string, error) {
270		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "worktree", "list", "--porcelain").Output()
271		if err != nil {
272			return nil, fmt.Errorf("git worktree list: %w", err)
273		}
274		var paths []string
275		for _, line := range strings.Split(string(out), "\n") {
276			if strings.HasPrefix(line, "worktree ") {
277				paths = append(paths, strings.TrimPrefix(line, "worktree "))
278			}
279		}
280		return paths, nil
281	}
282	
283	func (r *RealGitOps) WorktreePrune(dir string) error {
284		return internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "worktree", "prune").Run()
285	}

290		if err != nil {
291			return fmt.Errorf("git update-ref %s: %s: %w", ref, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
292		}
293		return nil
294	}

18	// ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime carries the non-serializable plumbing and
19	// per-invocation runtime intent for an execute-bead loop run. Durable knobs
20	// (assignee, retry caps, harness/model, tier bounds, etc.) live on
21	// config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via Run's rcfg argument.
22	type ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime struct {
23		Log          io.Writer
24		EventSink    io.Writer
25		ProgressCh   chan<- ProgressEvent
26		PreClaimHook func(ctx context.Context) error
27		// RoutePreflight, when non-nil, is invoked between nextCandidate and
28		// Claim. It is expected to call upstream ResolveRoute against the
29		// loop's resolved (harness, model) and return whatever typed routing
30		// error the upstream surfaced — notably agent.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible
31		// when the harness allow-list rejects the model. Any non-nil error
32		// causes the loop to exit immediately without claiming the bead, with
33		// a worker-level execution_failed record naming the rejected pair.
34		// DDx does NOT duplicate the upstream allow-list; this gate only
35		// consumes the typed-incompatibility surface.
36		RoutePreflight func(ctx context.Context, harness, model string) error
37		Once           bool
38		PollInterval   time.Duration
39		NoReview       bool
40		LabelFilter    string
41		SessionID      string
42		WorkerID       string
43		ProjectRoot    string
44	}
45	
46	// DefaultReviewMaxRetries is the number of reviewer attempts allowed per
47	// committed result_rev before the loop emits a terminal
48	// `review-manual-required` event and stops re-executing primary. FEAT-022 §14.
49	const DefaultReviewMaxRetries = 3
50	
51	// MaxLoopCooldown is the absolute upper bound the execute-loop will set for
52	// any execute-loop-retry-after value. Year-scale parks effectively mean
53	// "never retry" and that should be a deliberate operator choice via
54	// `ddx bead update --set execute-loop-retry-after=...`, not an automatic
55	// loop decision. Beyond this cap, the loop refuses to lengthen the cooldown
56	// further; an operator extending it manually still works.
57	const MaxLoopCooldown = 24 * time.Hour
58	
59	// LandConflictCooldown is the short cooldown applied when all conflict-recovery
60	// paths fail (3-way ort auto-merge + optional focused-resolve agent) and the
61	// bead is parked as land_conflict_unresolvable. Much shorter than
62	// MaxLoopCooldown because land conflicts often resolve quickly as sibling beads
63	// advance the base branch.
64	const LandConflictCooldown = 15 * time.Minute
65	
66	// StoreErrorCooldown is the short cooldown applied when a Store.* operation in
67	// the outcome-handling block returns a transient error. It prevents an
68	// immediate re-queue of the affected bead while allowing the loop to continue
69	// to the next candidate. Store errors during outcome handling are non-fatal:
70	// they are logged and emitted as loop-error events but do not terminate the
71	// worker.
72	const StoreErrorCooldown = 5 * time.Minute
73	
74	// handleOutcomeStoreError is called when a Store.* operation in the
75	// post-Execute outcome block returns a non-nil error. It logs the error,
76	// appends a kind:loop-error event to the bead (best-effort), sets a short
77	// cooldown so the bead is not immediately re-queued, increments
78	// result.Failures, and returns true — signalling the caller to continue to
79	// the next loop iteration rather than returning.
80	//
81	// If ctx is already cancelled, it returns false so the caller can propagate
82	// the cancellation immediately.
83	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) handleOutcomeStoreError(ctx context.Context, beadID, operation string, storeErr error, assignee string, result *ExecuteBeadLoopResult, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime, now func() time.Time) bool {
84		if ctx.Err() != nil {
85			return false
86		}
87		if runtime.Log != nil {
88			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "outcome store error (%s %s): %v (continuing)\n", operation, beadID, storeErr)
89		}
90		_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
91			Kind:      "loop-error",
92			Summary:   operation + " failed",
93			Body:      storeErr.Error(),
94			Actor:     assignee,
95			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
96			CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
97		})
98		cooldownUntil := now().UTC().Add(StoreErrorCooldown)
99		_ = w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(beadID, cooldownUntil, "loop-error", operation+": "+storeErr.Error())
100		result.Failures++

105	// capLoopCooldown clamps a loop-set cooldown duration to MaxLoopCooldown.
106	// The loop uses this for every SetExecutionCooldown call so no automatic
107	// path can silently park a bead for a year.
108	func capLoopCooldown(d time.Duration) time.Duration {
109		if d <= 0 {
110			return 0
111		}
112		if d > MaxLoopCooldown {
113			return MaxLoopCooldown
114		}
115		return d
116	}
