139		ReviewRationale string `json:"review_rationale,omitempty"`
140		// Tier is the model tier used for the final attempt (cheap, standard, smart).
141		// Populated by tier-escalating executors; empty for single-tier attempts.
142		Tier string `json:"tier,omitempty"`
143		// ProbeResult is a brief summary of the provider health probe at attempt time.
144		ProbeResult string `json:"probe_result,omitempty"`
145		// CostUSD is the dollar cost of this attempt as reported by the harness.
146		// Tier-escalating executors propagate this so the escalation trace can
147		// compute wasted/effective spend.
148		CostUSD float64 `json:"cost_usd,omitempty"`
149		// DurationMS is the wall-clock duration of this attempt.
150		DurationMS int64 `json:"duration_ms,omitempty"`
151		// Profile routing telemetry. Populated when execute-loop uses a profile
152		// ladder rather than an explicit harness/model pin.
153		RequestedProfile string `json:"requested_profile,omitempty"`
154		RequestedTier    string `json:"requested_tier,omitempty"`
155		ResolvedTier     string `json:"resolved_tier,omitempty"`
156		EscalationCount  int    `json:"escalation_count,omitempty"`
157		FinalTier        string `json:"final_tier,omitempty"`
158		// DecompositionRecommendation carries the structured list of recommended
159		// sub-bead titles when Status == declined_needs_decomposition. The loop
160		// records these on the bead as a `decomposition-recommendation` event so
161		// operators (or helix-evolve) can split the bead without re-deriving the
162		// recommendation.
163		DecompositionRecommendation []string `json:"decomposition_recommendation,omitempty"`
164		// DecompositionRationale is a free-form explanation accompanying
165		// DecompositionRecommendation. Optional.
166		DecompositionRationale string `json:"decomposition_rationale,omitempty"`
167	}

169	type ExecuteBeadExecutor interface {
170		Execute(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error)
171	}
172	
173	type ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error)
174	
175	func (f ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc) Execute(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
176		return f(ctx, beadID)
177	}
178	
179	// SatisfactionChecker evaluates whether a bead that returned no_changes is
180	// already satisfied and should be closed, or is still unresolved and should
181	// receive retry suppression. noChangesCount is the cumulative count including
182	// the current attempt.
183	//
184	// When satisfied is true the caller closes the bead with the returned evidence
185	// string as the detail. When false the caller applies a retry cooldown and
186	// leaves the bead open.
187	type SatisfactionChecker interface {
188		CheckSatisfied(ctx context.Context, beadID string, noChangesCount int) (satisfied bool, evidence string, err error)
189	}
190	
191	// SatisfactionCheckerFunc is a functional adapter for SatisfactionChecker.
192	type SatisfactionCheckerFunc func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, noChangesCount int) (bool, string, error)
193	
194	func (f SatisfactionCheckerFunc) CheckSatisfied(ctx context.Context, beadID string, noChangesCount int) (bool, string, error) {
195		return f(ctx, beadID, noChangesCount)
196	}
197	
198	type ExecuteBeadLoopStore interface {
199		ReadyExecution() ([]bead.Bead, error)
200		Claim(id, assignee string) error
201		Unclaim(id string) error
202		Heartbeat(id string) error
203		CloseWithEvidence(id, sessionID, commitSHA string) error
204		AppendEvent(id string, event bead.BeadEvent) error
205		Events(id string) ([]bead.BeadEvent, error)
206		SetExecutionCooldown(id string, until time.Time, status, detail string) error
207		AppendNotes(id string, notes string) error
208		IncrNoChangesCount(id string) (int, error)
209		// Reopen sets a closed bead back to open, appending notes to the bead's
210		// Notes field and recording a reopen event. Used by the post-merge review
211		// step when the reviewer returns REQUEST_CHANGES or BLOCK.
212		Reopen(id, reason, notes string) error
213	}
214	
215	// readyDiagnoser is the optional interface the work loop uses to explain an
216	// empty execution queue. bead.Store satisfies it via ReadyExecutionBreakdown.
217	type readyDiagnoser interface {
218		ReadyExecutionBreakdown() (bead.ReadyExecutionBreakdown, error)
219	}
220	
221	// NoReadyWorkBreakdown explains why the execution-ready queue is empty when
222	// dependency-ready beads exist. Populated on an ExecuteBeadLoopResult when
223	// NoReadyWork fires and the store exposes ReadyExecutionBreakdown.
224	type NoReadyWorkBreakdown struct {
225		SkippedEpics       []string `json:"skipped_epics,omitempty"`
226		SkippedOnCooldown  []string `json:"skipped_on_cooldown,omitempty"`
227		SkippedNotEligible []string `json:"skipped_not_eligible,omitempty"`
228		SkippedSuperseded  []string `json:"skipped_superseded,omitempty"`
229		NextRetryAfter     string   `json:"next_retry_after,omitempty"`
230	}
231	
232	// ProgressEvent is the FEAT-006 structured progress event. It is defined
233	// separately in the server package (server.ProgressEvent); this alias lets
234	// the agent package emit events without importing the server package.
235	// The field names and types are identical — the server package deserialises
236	// these directly from the channel.
237	//
238	// Terminal phases: done, preserved, failed.
239	type ProgressEvent struct {
240		EventID   string    `json:"event_id"`
241		AttemptID string    `json:"attempt_id"`
242		WorkerID  string    `json:"worker_id"`
243		ProjectID string    `json:"project_id"`
244		BeadID    string    `json:"bead_id"`
245		Harness   string    `json:"harness,omitempty"`
246		Model     string    `json:"model,omitempty"`
247		Profile   string    `json:"profile,omitempty"`
248		Phase     string    `json:"phase"`
249		PhaseSeq  int       `json:"phase_seq"`
250		Heartbeat bool      `json:"heartbeat"`
251		TS        time.Time `json:"ts"`
252		ElapsedMS int64     `json:"elapsed_ms"`
253		Message   string    `json:"message,omitempty"`
254	}
255	
256	type ExecuteBeadLoopResult struct {
257		Attempts          int                  `json:"attempts"`
258		Successes         int                  `json:"successes"`
259		Failures          int                  `json:"failures"`
260		NoReadyWork       bool                 `json:"no_ready_work,omitempty"`
261		NoReadyWorkDetail NoReadyWorkBreakdown `json:"no_ready_work_detail,omitempty"`
262		LastSuccessAt     time.Time            `json:"last_success_at,omitempty"`
263		LastFailureStatus string               `json:"last_failure_status,omitempty"`
264		Results           []ExecuteBeadReport  `json:"results,omitempty"`
265	}
266	
267	// ExecuteBeadWorker drains the current single-project execution-ready queue.
268	// It intentionally does not retry a failed/conflicted bead again in the same
269	// process run; a later operator-driven invocation can create the next attempt.
270	type ExecuteBeadWorker struct {
271		Store               ExecuteBeadLoopStore
272		Executor            ExecuteBeadExecutor
273		SatisfactionChecker SatisfactionChecker // nil → count-based default
274		Now                 func() time.Time
275		// Reviewer, when non-nil, is called after every successful merge to
276		// validate the commit against the bead's acceptance criteria. When nil,
277		// post-merge review is skipped (same behaviour as --no-review).
278		Reviewer BeadReviewer
279	
280		// ConflictResolver, when non-nil, is called after the 3-way ort auto-merge
281		// step fails to recover a preserved iteration. The implementation should
282		// attempt a focused conflict-resolution pass (e.g. a cheap-tier agent run
283		// with the conflict files and bead AC) and return the new merged tip SHA on
284		// success. isBlocking=true signals the conflict requires human judgment
285		// (escalating to land_conflict_needs_human); false means failed-but-retriable.
286		ConflictResolver func(ctx context.Context, beadID, preserveRef, projectRoot string) (newTip string, isBlocking bool, err error)
287	
288		// ComplexityGate, when non-nil, is invoked between RoutePreflight and Claim.
289		// It evaluates whether the candidate bead is atomic (proceed to Claim),
290		// decomposable (file children, block parent, re-pick), or ambiguous
291		// (surface to human triage). When nil, the gate is bypassed and a one-time
292		// warning is emitted per Run() invocation. See triage.go and
293		// NewComplexityGate for the standard implementation.
294		ComplexityGate TriageGate
295	
296		// conflictAutoRecoverFn replaces the default landConflictAutoRecover. Set
297		// in tests to inject controlled recovery results without a real git repo.
298		conflictAutoRecoverFn func(wd, preserveRef string, gitOps LandingGitOps) (string, error)
299	}

18	// loadExecutionsMirrorConfig reads .ddx/config.yaml at projectRoot and returns
19	// the executions.mirror block when configured. Errors return nil silently —
20	// missing or invalid mirror config simply disables mirroring; it never blocks
21	// execute-bead.
22	func loadExecutionsMirrorConfig(projectRoot string) *config.ExecutionsMirrorConfig {
23		cfg, err := config.LoadWithWorkingDir(projectRoot)
24		if err != nil || cfg == nil || cfg.Executions == nil || cfg.Executions.Mirror == nil {
25			return nil
26		}
27		return cfg.Executions.Mirror
28	}

39	func (f *CommandFactory) newAgentExecuteBeadCommand() *cobra.Command {
40		cmd := &cobra.Command{
41			Use:   "execute-bead <bead-id>",
42			Short: "Run an agent on one bead in an isolated worktree, then land the result",
43			Long: `execute-bead is the primitive: it runs a single agent on a single bead in
44	an isolated git worktree (the worker step), then hands the result to the
45	parent-side orchestrator which merges or preserves the commits.
46	
47	The worker classifies its outcome as one of:
48	  task_succeeded  — agent exited 0 and produced commits
49	  task_no_changes — agent exited 0 but made no commits
50	  task_failed     — agent exited non-zero
51	
52	The orchestrator then applies landing logic (merge, gate evaluation, preserve)
53	and the final result reflects both the worker and landing decisions.
54	
55	For normal queue-driven work use "ddx agent execute-loop", which claims
56	ready beads, calls this command, and closes or unclaims each bead based on
57	the result. Reach for execute-bead directly only to debug or re-run a
58	specific bead.
59	
60	Result status is reported on the "status:" line and is one of:
61	  success                          — merged (or preserved with --no-merge)
62	  no_changes                       — agent ran but produced no diff
63	  no_evidence_produced             — agent exited with no commit and no rationale
64	  already_satisfied                — closed by the loop after repeated no_changes
65	  land_conflict                    — merge failed; result preserved
66	  post_run_check_failed            — checks failed; result preserved
67	  execution_failed                 — agent or harness error
68	  structural_validation_failed     — bead or prompt inputs invalid
69	
70	execute-loop closes the bead with session/commit evidence only on success
71	(and already_satisfied); every other status leaves the bead open and
72	unclaimed for a later attempt.`,
73			Example: `  # Debug one specific bead (prefer "execute-loop" for normal queue work)
74	  ddx agent execute-bead ddx-7a01ba6c
75	
76	  # Run against a non-HEAD base revision
77	  ddx agent execute-bead ddx-7a01ba6c --from main
78	
79	  # Preserve the result under refs/ddx/iterations/... instead of merging
80	  ddx agent execute-bead ddx-7a01ba6c --no-merge
81	
82	  # Pin harness/model for a debugging pass
83	  ddx agent execute-bead ddx-7a01ba6c --harness codex`,
84			Args: cobra.ExactArgs(1),
85			RunE: f.runAgentExecuteBead,
86		}
87		cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Project root path (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT")
88		cmd.Flags().String("from", "", "Base git revision to start from (default: HEAD)")
89		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-merge", false, "Skip merge; preserve result under refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/<timestamp>-<base-shortsha> instead")
90		cmd.Flags().String("harness", "", "Agent harness to use")
91		cmd.Flags().String("model", "", "Model override")
92		cmd.Flags().String("provider", "", "Provider name (e.g. vidar, openrouter); selects a named provider from config")
93		cmd.Flags().String("model-ref", "", "Model catalog reference (e.g. code-medium); resolved via the model catalog")
94		cmd.Flags().String("effort", "", "Effort level")
95		cmd.Flags().String("context-budget", "", "Context budget for prompt: empty (full), minimal (omit large governing docs for cheap-tier)")
96		cmd.Flags().String("prompt", "", "Prompt file path (auto-generated from bead if omitted)")
97		cmd.Flags().Duration("request-timeout", 0, "Per-request provider wall-clock timeout (e.g. 60m, 2h); overrides project config and model-class defaults. Use when a thinking model needs more than the default 15 min.")
98		cmd.Flags().Int("min-power", 0, "Minimum model power required (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
99		cmd.Flags().Int("max-power", 0, "Maximum model power allowed (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
100		cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "Output result as JSON")
101		return cmd
102	}
103	
104	func (f *CommandFactory) runAgentExecuteBead(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
105		beadID := args[0]
106		if !validBeadID.MatchString(beadID) {
107			return fmt.Errorf("invalid bead ID %q: must contain only letters, digits, dots, underscores, and hyphens", beadID)
108		}
109		projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
110		fromRev, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("from")
111		noMerge, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-merge")
112		harness, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("harness")
113		model, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model")
114		provider, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("provider")
115		modelRef, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model-ref")
116		effort, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("effort")
117		contextBudget, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("context-budget")
118		promptFile, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("prompt")
119		requestTimeout, _ := cmd.Flags().GetDuration("request-timeout")
120		minPower, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("min-power")
121		maxPower, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("max-power")
122		asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
123	
124		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
125	
126		overrides := config.CLIOverrides{
127			Harness:       harness,
128			Model:         model,
129			Provider:      provider,
130			ModelRef:      modelRef,
131			Effort:        effort,
132			ContextBudget: contextBudget,
133			MinPower:      minPower,
134			MaxPower:      maxPower,
135		}
136		if requestTimeout > 0 {
137			overrides.ProviderRequestTimeout = &requestTimeout
138		}
139		rcfg, err := config.LoadAndResolve(projectRoot, overrides)
140		if err != nil {
141			return fmt.Errorf("load config at %s: %w", projectRoot, err)
142		}
143		runtime := agent.ExecuteBeadRuntime{
144			FromRev:    fromRev,
145			PromptFile: promptFile,
146			WorkerID:   os.Getenv("DDX_WORKER_ID"),
147			BeadEvents: bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")),
148		}
149	
150		var gitOps agent.GitOps = &agent.RealGitOps{}
151		if f.executeBeadGitOverride != nil {
152			gitOps = f.executeBeadGitOverride
153		}
154	
155		var orchestratorGitOps agent.OrchestratorGitOps = &agent.RealOrchestratorGitOps{}
156		if f.executeBeadOrchestratorGitOverride != nil {
157			orchestratorGitOps = f.executeBeadOrchestratorGitOverride
158		}
159	
160		// Test injection seam: AgentRunnerOverride feeds canned *Result values
161		// from fakes without spinning up a real provider. Production callers
162		// leave this nil so ExecuteBead constructs an agent service from
163		// projectRoot internally.
164		if f.AgentRunnerOverride != nil {
165			runtime.AgentRunner = f.AgentRunnerOverride
166		}
167	
168		// Preflight the orphan-model check before creating a worktree. Mirrors
169		// the execute-loop preflight so `ddx agent execute-bead --model <unroutable>`
170		// errors before any git work happens rather than mid-execution. Skip the
171		// preflight when a test runner override is in use — overrides may be
172		// fakes that bypass routing entirely.
173		if f.AgentRunnerOverride == nil {
174			if err := agent.ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService(cmd.Context(), f.WorkingDir, harness, model, provider, modelRef); err != nil {
175				return err
176			}
177		}
178	
179		// Recover any orphaned worktrees from previous crashed runs before
180		// spawning a new worker. This is the parent's responsibility.
181		agent.RecoverOrphans(gitOps, projectRoot, beadID)
182	
183		// Worker step: run the agent in an isolated worktree.
184		res, err := agent.ExecuteBeadWithConfig(cmd.Context(), projectRoot, beadID, rcfg, runtime, gitOps)
185		if err != nil && res == nil {
186			return err
187		}
188	
189		// Orchestrator step: land the result (ff or merge → push, gate eval,
190		// preserve). Always run when we have a result — the orchestrator handles
191		// all cases including no-changes and error (agent failed with no commits).
192		//
193		// Single-bead CLI is strictly serial (no sibling workers in this
194		// process), so we can wire the LandingAdvancer to call Land() directly
195		// without going through a per-project coordinator goroutine. Tests may
196		// inject a custom advancer via f.executeBeadLandingAdvancerOverride.
197		if res != nil {
198			var advancer func(r *agent.ExecuteBeadResult) (*agent.LandResult, error)
199			if f.executeBeadLandingAdvancerOverride != nil {
200				advancer = f.executeBeadLandingAdvancerOverride

1	// Package routinglint implements the FEAT-006 routing-cleanup lint
2	// rule that mechanically forbids reintroduction of DDx-side
3	// "compensating routing" logic that was deleted with prejudice in
4	// ddx-3bd7396a.
5	//
6	// The parent epic ddx-fdd3ea36 §AC#7 says:
7	//
8	//	"Search DDx for code that re-implements upstream routing
9	//	concerns: allow-list checks, exact-pin filtering,
10	//	profile-to-preference mapping, provider cost scoring. Must be
11	//	absent on the default path. ... Dead-code detector run in CI
12	//	catches orphans."
13	//
14	// Parent-epic AC#7 originally expected the helpers to remain present,
15	// reachable only via --escalate. The follow-up bead ddx-3bd7396a
16	// instead deleted them entirely, so this analyzer enforces the
17	// stricter invariant: the listed compensating-routing identifiers and
18	// flag literals must not reappear anywhere in cli/ Go source. If they
19	// do, the cleanup has regressed and CI fails.
20	//
21	// Forbidden identifiers (exact name match):
22	//
23	//	ResolveProfileLadder, ResolveTierModelRef, AdaptiveMinTier,
24	//	workersByHarness, ResolveProfileLadderCallCount.
25	//
26	// Forbidden string literals (anywhere in cli/ Go source):
27	//
28	//	"--escalate", "--override-model",
29	//	"profile_ladders", "model_overrides",
30	//	"agent.routing.profile_ladders",
31	//	"agent.routing.model_overrides".
32	//
33	// String-literal occurrences inside this analyzer's own package and
34	// inside testdata fixtures are exempt.
35	//
36	// A specific occurrence may be intentionally exempted (e.g. the
37	// migration code that rejects a config containing
38	// agent.routing.profile_ladders has to name the literal key) by
39	// adding a
40	//
41	//	// routinglint:legacy-rejection reason="..."
42	//
43	// comment on the same line as the offending node, or on a comment
44	// line directly above it. The reason= clause must be present and
45	// non-empty.
46	package routinglint
47	
48	import (
49		"go/ast"
50		"go/token"
51		"regexp"
52		"strings"
53	
54		"golang.org/x/tools/go/analysis"
55		"golang.org/x/tools/go/analysis/passes/inspect"
56		"golang.org/x/tools/go/ast/inspector"
57	)
58	
59	const exemptionAnnotation = "routinglint:legacy-rejection"
60	
61	var exemptionRE = regexp.MustCompile(`routinglint:legacy-rejection\s+reason="[^"]+"`)
62	
63	// Analyzer is the FEAT-006 routing-cleanup analyzer.
64	var Analyzer = &analysis.Analyzer{
65		Name:     "routinglint",
66		Doc:      "flags reintroduction of compensating DDx-side routing helpers and flags retired by FEAT-006 / ddx-3bd7396a",
67		Requires: []*analysis.Analyzer{inspect.Analyzer},
68		Run:      run,
69	}
70	
71	// forbiddenIdents is the closed list of compensating-routing
72	// identifiers retired by ddx-3bd7396a. Reintroduction is a CI failure.
73	var forbiddenIdents = map[string]string{
74		"ResolveProfileLadder":          "tier-ladder resolver retired by ddx-3bd7396a — DDx must not re-implement profile-to-preference mapping",
75		"ResolveTierModelRef":           "tier-model resolver retired by ddx-3bd7396a — DDx must not re-implement exact-pin filtering",
76		"ResolveProfileLadderCallCount": "test seam for the retired tier-ladder resolver — also retired by ddx-3bd7396a",
77		"AdaptiveMinTier":               "adaptive-min-tier promotion logic retired by ddx-3bd7396a",
78		"workersByHarness":              "escalation worker grouping helper retired by ddx-3bd7396a",
79	}
80	
81	// forbiddenLiterals is the closed list of CLI flag and config-key
82	// strings retired by ddx-3bd7396a. They must not appear as Go string
83	// literals anywhere in cli/.
84	var forbiddenLiterals = map[string]string{
85		"--escalate":                    "--escalate flag retired by ddx-3bd7396a — escalation is not a DDx-side concern",
86		"--override-model":              "--override-model flag retired by ddx-3bd7396a — model overrides go through --model on the default path",
87		"profile_ladders":               "agent.routing.profile_ladders config key retired by ddx-3bd7396a",
88		"model_overrides":               "agent.routing.model_overrides config key retired by ddx-3bd7396a",
89		"agent.routing.profile_ladders": "agent.routing.profile_ladders config key retired by ddx-3bd7396a",
90		"agent.routing.model_overrides": "agent.routing.model_overrides config key retired by ddx-3bd7396a",
91	}
92	
93	// selfPkgFragments lists path fragments that mark this analyzer's own
94	// package or its testdata fixtures. Diagnostics from those packages
95	// are suppressed because the closed-list constants above legitimately
96	// embed every forbidden token.
97	var selfPkgFragments = []string{
98		"/tools/lint/routinglint",
99		"tools/lint/routinglint",
100	}
101	
102	func inSelfPkg(pass *analysis.Pass) bool {
103		if pass.Pkg == nil {
104			return false
105		}
106		p := pass.Pkg.Path()
107		for _, f := range selfPkgFragments {
108			if strings.Contains(p, f) {
109				return true
110			}
111		}
112		return false
113	}
114	
115	func run(pass *analysis.Pass) (interface{}, error) {
116		if inSelfPkg(pass) {
117			return nil, nil
118		}
119	
120		insp := pass.ResultOf[inspect.Analyzer].(*inspector.Inspector)
121	
122		// Build a per-file index of lines that carry an exemption
123		// annotation, either as an inline comment on the same line or as
124		// a stand-alone comment line directly above. The annotation
125		// covers the line it sits on plus the line immediately following.
126		exempted := make(map[token.Position]bool)
127		for _, f := range pass.Files {
128			for _, cg := range f.Comments {
129				for _, c := range cg.List {
130					if !strings.Contains(c.Text, exemptionAnnotation) {
131						continue
132					}
133					if !exemptionRE.MatchString(c.Text) {
134						pass.Report(analysis.Diagnostic{
135							Pos:     c.Pos(),
136							Message: `routinglint: ` + exemptionAnnotation + ` annotation is missing a non-empty reason="..." clause`,
137						})
138						continue
139					}
140					cp := pass.Fset.Position(c.Pos())
141					exempted[token.Position{Filename: cp.Filename, Line: cp.Line}] = true
142					exempted[token.Position{Filename: cp.Filename, Line: cp.Line + 1}] = true
143				}
144			}
145		}
146	
147		isExempt := func(pos token.Pos) bool {
148			p := pass.Fset.Position(pos)
149			return exempted[token.Position{Filename: p.Filename, Line: p.Line}]
150		}
151	
152		report := func(pos token.Pos, msg string) {
153			if isExempt(pos) {
154				return
155			}
156			pass.Report(analysis.Diagnostic{Pos: pos, Message: msg})
157		}
158	
159		// Forbidden identifiers — flag at every occurrence, both
160		// declarations and references. Matching is exact on Ident.Name so
161		// substrings inside larger identifiers (e.g. the test name
162		// TestExecuteLoopLocalRejectsProfileLadders) do not trip the lint.
163		insp.Preorder([]ast.Node{(*ast.Ident)(nil)}, func(n ast.Node) {
164			id := n.(*ast.Ident)
165			if reason, ok := forbiddenIdents[id.Name]; ok {
166				report(id.Pos(), "routinglint: forbidden identifier "+id.Name+" — "+reason)
167			}
168		})
169	
170		// Forbidden string literals — flag the literal node itself.
171		insp.Preorder([]ast.Node{(*ast.BasicLit)(nil)}, func(n ast.Node) {
172			lit := n.(*ast.BasicLit)
173			if lit.Kind != token.STRING {
174				return
175			}
176			// Trim surrounding quote/backtick so we compare raw value.
177			s := lit.Value
178			if len(s) >= 2 && (s[0] == '"' || s[0] == '`') {
179				s = s[1 : len(s)-1]
180			}
181			if reason, ok := forbiddenLiterals[s]; ok {
182				report(lit.Pos(), "routinglint: forbidden string literal "+lit.Value+" — "+reason)
183			}
184		})
185	
186		return nil, nil
187	}

1600	func (f *CommandFactory) newAgentExecuteLoopCommand() *cobra.Command {
1601		cmd := &cobra.Command{
1602			Use:   "execute-loop",
1603			Short: "Drain the single-project execution-ready bead queue",
1604			Long: `execute-loop is the primary queue-driven execution surface. It scans the
1605	target project's execution-ready bead queue, claims the next ready bead,
1606	runs "ddx agent execute-bead" on it from the project root, records the
1607	structured result, and continues until no unattempted ready work remains.
1608	
1609	Reach for execute-loop by default. Use "ddx agent execute-bead" directly
1610	only as the primitive for debugging or re-running one specific bead.
1611	
1612	Planning and document-only beads are valid execution targets — any bead
1613	with unmet acceptance criteria and no blocking deps is eligible.
1614	
1615	Close semantics (per execute-bead result status):
1616	  success                      — close bead with session + commit evidence
1617	  already_satisfied            — close bead (after repeated no_changes)
1618	  no_changes                   — unclaim; may cooldown or close after retries
1619	  land_conflict                — unclaim; result preserved under refs/ddx/iterations/
1620	  post_run_check_failed        — unclaim; result preserved
1621	  execution_failed             — unclaim
1622	  structural_validation_failed — unclaim
1623	
1624	Only success (and already_satisfied) closes the bead. Every other status
1625	leaves the bead open and unclaimed so a later attempt can try again. Each
1626	attempt is appended to the bead as an execute-bead event (status, detail,
1627	base_rev, result_rev, preserve_ref, retry_after), and the underlying agent
1628	session log is recorded under the execute-bead agent-log path.
1629	
1630	By default execute-loop submits to the running ddx server as a background
1631	worker and returns immediately. Use --local to run inline in the current
1632	process.
1633	
1634	Project targeting (multi-project servers):
1635	  --project <path>    target a specific project root (absolute path or name)
1636	  DDX_PROJECT_ROOT    env var fallback; used when --project is not set
1637	  (default)           the git root of the current working directory
1638	
1639	When submitting to a multi-project server you must ensure the target project
1640	is registered with the server (run "ddx server" from that directory, or use
1641	"ddx server projects register"). The server rejects unrecognised project paths.
1642	`,
1643			Example: `  # Drain the current execution-ready queue once and exit (normal surface)
1644	  ddx agent execute-loop
1645	
1646	  # Pick one ready bead, execute it, and stop
1647	  ddx agent execute-loop --profile default --once
1648	
1649	  # Run continuously as a bounded queue worker
1650	  ddx agent execute-loop --poll-interval 30s
1651	
1652	  # Force a specific harness/model for a debugging pass
1653	  ddx agent execute-loop --once --harness codex
1654	  ddx agent execute-loop --once --harness agent --model minimax/minimax-m2.7
1655	
1656	  # Run inline in the current process (not recommended for long runs)
1657	  ddx agent execute-loop --local --once`,
1658			Args: cobra.NoArgs,
1659			RunE: f.runAgentExecuteLoop,
1660		}
1661		cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Target project root path or name (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT")
1662		cmd.Flags().String("from", "", "Base git revision to start from (default: HEAD)")
1663		cmd.Flags().String("harness", "", "Agent harness to use")
1664		cmd.Flags().String("model", "", "Model override")
1665		cmd.Flags().String("profile", agent.DefaultRoutingProfile, "Routing profile: default, cheap, fast, or smart")

2288	// modelPassthroughCapture is a passthrough wrapper around an agent.AgentRunner
2289	// that records the most recent RunArgs before forwarding to the underlying
2290	// runner. It instruments TestExecuteBeadModelFlagPassthrough so the test can
2291	// assert what ExecuteBead handed the runner without itself faking the runner —
2292	// every Run call is forwarded to the real script harness so production routing
2293	// semantics still execute end-to-end.
2294	type modelPassthroughCapture struct {
2295		inner agent.AgentRunner
2296		last  agent.RunArgs
2297	}
2298	
2299	func (c *modelPassthroughCapture) Run(opts agent.RunArgs) (*agent.Result, error) {
2300		c.last = opts
2301		return c.inner.Run(opts)
2302	}

2304	// TestExecuteBeadModelFlagPassthrough locks in the resolution contract for
2305	// execute-bead's model option: the value supplied via ExecuteBeadOptions.Model
2306	// is passed verbatim to the runner, and an empty value is not silently replaced
2307	// by any hardcoded or catalog-derived default. This regression test guards
2308	// against routing layers injecting a model (e.g. a stale vendor/model like
2309	// "z-ai/glm-5.1") when the caller did not request one — the case the agent
2310	// harness resolves from ~/.config/agent/config.yaml must be preserved by
2311	// ExecuteBead handing the runner an empty Model so the harness's own
2312	// resolution chain runs.
2313	//
2314	// The test runs against a real git repo and the script harness (wrapped in a
2315	// passthrough capture so the test can observe opts.Model). The script harness
2316	// resolves its directive file from opts.PromptFile when opts.Model is empty or
2317	// not a readable file, so both the empty-model and the explicit-non-path-model
2318	// subtests exercise the real runner end-to-end.
2319	func TestExecuteBeadModelFlagPassthrough(t *testing.T) {
2320		scrubEnv := func() []string {

19	func TestDrainServiceEventsNoopCompactionWallClockBreaker(t *testing.T) {
20		events := make(chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, 400)
21		start := time.Date(2026, 4, 19, 12, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC)
22		for elapsed := time.Duration(0); elapsed <= serviceNoopCompactionWallClockLimit; elapsed += 3 * time.Second {
23			events <- noopCompactionServiceEvent(start.Add(elapsed))
24		}
25		close(events)
26	
27		final, _, _, _ := drainServiceEvents(events)
28		require.NotNil(t, final)
29		assert.Equal(t, "stalled", final.Status)
30		assert.Contains(t, final.Error, serviceNoopCompactionWallClockReason)
31		assert.Contains(t, final.Error, "time-based breaker")
32		assert.Contains(t, final.Error, "15m0s")
33	}
34	
35	func TestDrainServiceEventsProgressResetsNoopCompactionWallClockBreaker(t *testing.T) {
36		events := make(chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, 700)
37		start := time.Date(2026, 4, 19, 12, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC)
38		for elapsed := time.Duration(0); elapsed < serviceNoopCompactionWallClockLimit; elapsed += 3 * time.Second {
39			events <- noopCompactionServiceEvent(start.Add(elapsed))
40		}
41		events <- agentlib.ServiceEvent{
42			Type: "tool_call",
43			Time: start.Add(serviceNoopCompactionWallClockLimit),
44			Data: json.RawMessage(`{"id":"call-1","name":"read","input":{"path":"README.md"}}`),
45		}
46		for elapsed := 3 * time.Second; elapsed < serviceNoopCompactionWallClockLimit; elapsed += 3 * time.Second {
47			events <- noopCompactionServiceEvent(start.Add(serviceNoopCompactionWallClockLimit + elapsed))
48		}
49		events <- agentlib.ServiceEvent{
50			Type: "final",
51			Time: start.Add(2 * serviceNoopCompactionWallClockLimit),
52			Data: json.RawMessage(`{"status":"success","exit_code":0,"duration_ms":1}`),
53		}
54		close(events)
55	
56		final, _, _, _ := drainServiceEvents(events)
57		require.NotNil(t, final)
58		assert.Equal(t, "success", final.Status)
59		assert.Empty(t, final.Error)
60	}

63		const beadID = "ddx-compaction-stuck"
64	
65		projectRoot := setupArtifactTestProjectRoot(t)
66		gitOps := &artifactTestGitOps{
67			projectRoot: projectRoot,
68			baseRev:     "aaaa000000000001",

70			wtSetupFn: func(wtPath string) {
71				setupArtifactTestWorktree(t, wtPath, beadID, "", false, 0)
72			},
73		}
74

570	// Merge, UpdateRef, gate evaluation, preserve-ref management, and orphan
571	// recovery are the parent's responsibility (see LandBeadResult, RecoverOrphans).
572	//
573	// Agent dispatch: production callers leave runtime.Service and runtime.AgentRunner
574	// nil. ExecuteBeadWithConfig constructs a fresh agentlib.DdxAgent from
575	// projectRoot via NewServiceFromWorkDir and dispatches via RunViaServiceWith.
576	// Tests may set runtime.AgentRunner to inject a fake that returns canned
577	// Result values; when set, it takes precedence over the service path.
578	func ExecuteBeadWithConfig(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string, beadID string, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime ExecuteBeadRuntime, gitOps GitOps) (*ExecuteBeadResult, error) {
579		if ctx == nil {
580			ctx = context.Background()
581		}
582		attemptID := GenerateAttemptID()
583		if runtime.WorkerID == "" {
584			runtime.WorkerID = os.Getenv("DDX_WORKER_ID")
585		}
586	
587		wtPath := executeBeadWorktreePath(beadID, attemptID)
588		if mkErr := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(wtPath), 0o755); mkErr != nil {
589			return nil, fmt.Errorf("creating execute-bead worktree parent dir: %w", mkErr)
590		}
591	
592		// Commit beads.jsonl before spawning worktree so the worktree snapshot
593		// includes any bead metadata updates (e.g. spec-id).
594		if err := CommitTracker(projectRoot); err != nil {
595			return nil, err
596		}
597	
598		// Checkpoint any remaining caller dirt as a real commit on the current
599		// branch (FEAT-012 §22, US-126 AC#1). The new HEAD becomes the effective
600		// base for the worker worktree; caller's edits are preserved as a normal
601		// commit they can `git reset HEAD~` if they want them back uncommitted.
602		if _, err := gitOps.SynthesizeCommit(projectRoot, "chore: checkpoint pre-execute-bead "+attemptID); err != nil {
603			return nil, fmt.Errorf("pre-execute-bead checkpoint: %w", err)
604		}
605	
606		// Resolve base revision after the tracker + checkpoint commits.
607		baseRev, err := resolveBase(gitOps, projectRoot, runtime.FromRev)
608		if err != nil {
609			return nil, err
610		}
611	
612		// Create the isolated worktree. Orphan recovery is the parent's responsibility
613		// (call RecoverOrphans before invoking workers).
614		if err := gitOps.WorktreeAdd(projectRoot, wtPath, baseRev); err != nil {
615			return nil, fmt.Errorf("creating isolated worktree: %w", err)
616		}
617		defer func() {
618			_ = gitOps.WorktreeRemove(projectRoot, wtPath)
619		}()
620	
621		// Publish the live run-state so operators and HELIX can observe what is
622		// executing without polling the bead tracker (CONTRACT-001 §5). The file
623		// is removed on completion; a crashed worker leaves a stale file that
624		// RecoverOrphans sweeps before the next attempt.
625		_ = WriteRunState(projectRoot, RunState{
626			BeadID:       beadID,
627			AttemptID:    attemptID,
628			Harness:      rcfg.Harness(),
629			Model:        rcfg.Model(),
630			StartedAt:    time.Now().UTC(),
631			WorktreePath: wtPath,
632		})
633		defer func() {
634			_ = ClearRunState(projectRoot)
635		}()
636	
637		// Repair project-local skill symlinks whose targets do not resolve inside
638		// the freshly created worktree.
639		_ = materializeWorktreeSkills(wtPath)
640	
641		// Prepare artifacts (context load, prompt generation).
642		artifacts, err := prepareArtifacts(projectRoot, wtPath, beadID, attemptID, baseRev, rcfg, runtime)
643		if err != nil {
644			res := &ExecuteBeadResult{
645				BeadID:    beadID,
646				AttemptID: attemptID,
647				WorkerID:  runtime.WorkerID,
648				BaseRev:   baseRev,
649				ResultRev: baseRev, // no commits; ResultRev == BaseRev signals no output
650				ExitCode:  1,
651				Error:     err.Error(),
652				Outcome:   ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed,
653			}
654			if abInfo, _ := os.Stat(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ExecuteBeadArtifactDir, attemptID)); abInfo != nil && abInfo.IsDir() {
655				res.ExecutionDir = filepath.Join(ExecuteBeadArtifactDir, attemptID)
656			}
657			res.FailureMode = ClassifyFailureMode(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, res.Error)
658			populateWorkerStatus(res)
659			_ = writeArtifactJSON(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ExecuteBeadArtifactDir, attemptID, "result.json"), res)
660			return res, fmt.Errorf("execute-bead context load: %w", err)
661		}
662	
663		// Pre-create the execution bundle dir in the worktree so the agent can write
664		// artifacts (e.g. no_changes_rationale.txt) without needing to create the
665		// directory itself. Failures are non-fatal: the agent can create it on demand.
666		_ = os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(wtPath, artifacts.DirRel), 0o755)
667	
668		// Redirect per-run session/telemetry output into the DDx-owned execution
669		// bundle so the embedded harness does not accumulate state at the worktree root.

39	// Resolve produces a sealed ResolvedConfig by layering overrides onto

41	// returned ResolvedConfig does not alias cfg's storage — every map
42	// and slice is deep-cloned via per-type Clone methods before being
43	// captured.
44	//
45	// Resolve accepts a nil cfg and returns a ResolvedConfig populated
46	// from package defaults, sealed normally.
47	func (c *NewConfig) Resolve(overrides CLIOverrides) ResolvedConfig {
48		r := ResolvedConfig{sealed: true}
49	
50		r.assignee = overrides.Assignee

121	// LoadAndResolve is the canonical production entry point that produces
122	// a sealed ResolvedConfig. It loads the project's .ddx/config.yaml from
123	// projectRoot, layers overrides on top, and returns the immutable
124	// ResolvedConfig.
125	//
126	// On load error, LoadAndResolve still returns a usable, sealed
127	// ResolvedConfig populated from package defaults plus the supplied
128	// overrides, alongside the underlying error. Callers decide whether to
129	// surface the error or proceed with the defaults-backed config.
130	//

132	func LoadAndResolve(projectRoot string, overrides CLIOverrides) (ResolvedConfig, error) {
133		cfg, err := LoadWithWorkingDir(projectRoot)

1	package agent
2	
3	import "strings"
4	
5	// PushFailedReasonPrefix is the canonical Reason prefix written by the
6	// landing layer when `git push` rejects the new tip after a local merge or
7	// fast-forward succeeded. ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus matches on this prefix
8	// to map a merged-locally-but-not-pushed outcome to push_failed instead of
9	// success, so the bead stays open.
10	const PushFailedReasonPrefix = "landed locally; push failed:"
11	
12	// PushConflictReasonPrefix marks a more specific failure mode than
13	// PushFailedReasonPrefix: the local push was rejected because the remote
14	// advanced, the loop attempted an automatic pull/merge to recover, and the
15	// merge produced a conflict the loop cannot resolve. The bead is parked for
16	// human review with a short cooldown rather than a year-long park.
17	const PushConflictReasonPrefix = "landed locally; push conflict:"
18	
19	// FailureMode constants classify *why* an execution did not land cleanly.
20	// They are orthogonal to Outcome/Status: a single failed attempt carries one
21	// outcome (e.g. preserved) and one failure mode (e.g. merge_conflict) so
22	// measurement surfaces can aggregate failures by cause.
23	const (
24		FailureModeContextOverflow     = "context_overflow"
25		FailureModeMergeConflict       = "merge_conflict"
26		FailureModeTestFailure         = "test_failure"
27		FailureModeBuildFailure        = "build_failure"
28		FailureModeTimeout             = "timeout"
29		FailureModeAuthError           = "auth_error"
30		FailureModeNoChanges           = "no_changes"
31		FailureModeNoEvidenceProduced  = "no_evidence_produced"
32		FailureModeRatchetMiss         = "ratchet_miss"
33		FailureModeNoViableProvider    = "no_viable_provider"
34		FailureModeHarnessNotInstalled = "harness_not_installed"
35		FailureModeUnknown             = "unknown"
36	)
37	
38	// ClassifyFailureMode derives a failure_mode for a worker-level result from
39	// its outcome, exit code, and error message. Returns "" when the outcome is
40	// task_succeeded (success carries no failure mode). task_no_changes always
41	// maps to FailureModeNoChanges regardless of error text.
42	//
43	// For task_failed (or non-zero exit code), the error message is scanned for
44	// well-known substrings. The order matters: context/auth/timeout patterns
45	// are checked before generic test/build patterns so a "test timed out"
46	// classifies as timeout rather than test_failure. Anything unrecognised
47	// maps to FailureModeUnknown so aggregates never hide a missed pattern.
48	func ClassifyFailureMode(outcome string, exitCode int, errMsg string) string {
49		switch outcome {
50		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded:
51			if exitCode == 0 {
52				return ""
53			}
54			// Degenerate: success outcome with non-zero exit. Treat as unknown
55			// failure rather than success so the aggregate flags the anomaly.
56		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
57			return FailureModeNoChanges
58		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence:
59			return FailureModeNoEvidenceProduced
60		}
61	
62		lower := strings.ToLower(errMsg)
63		switch {
64		case containsAny(lower,
65			"executable file not found in $path",
66			"executable file not found",
67			"no such file or directory") &&
68			containsAny(lower, "exec:", "harness", "claude", "agent", "codex", "gemini"):
69			return FailureModeHarnessNotInstalled
70		case containsAny(lower,
71			"no viable harness",
72			"no harness configured",
73			"failed to initialize routing service",
74			"no viable provider"):
75			return FailureModeNoViableProvider
76		case containsAny(lower,
77			"context length",
78			"context_length_exceeded",
79			"context window",
80			"maximum context",
81			"prompt is too long",
82			"prompt too long",
83			"token limit",
84			"too many tokens",
85			"exceeds the context"):
86			return FailureModeContextOverflow
87		case containsAny(lower,
88			"unauthorized",
89			"401 unauthorized",
90			"invalid api key",
91			"invalid_api_key",
92			"authentication failed",
93			"authentication_error",
94			"no api key",
95			"missing api key",
96			"quota exceeded",
97			"insufficient quota",
98			"insufficient_quota",
99			"rate limit",
100			"rate_limit",
101			"429"):
102			return FailureModeAuthError
103		case containsAny(lower,
104			"timeout",
105			"timed out",
106			"context deadline exceeded",
107			"deadline exceeded",
108			"signal: killed"):
109			return FailureModeTimeout
110		case containsAny(lower,
111			"merge conflict",
112			"ff-merge not possible",
113			"merge failed",
114			"automatic merge failed"):
115			return FailureModeMergeConflict
116		case containsAny(lower,
117			"build failed",
118			"compilation failed",
119			"compile error",
120			"compilation error",
121			"cannot find package",
122			"undefined:",
123			"undefined reference",
124			"syntax error"):
125			return FailureModeBuildFailure
126		case containsAny(lower,
127			"test failed",
128			"tests failed",
129			"--- fail:",
130			"assertion failed",
131			"test failure"):
132			return FailureModeTestFailure
133		}
134		if exitCode != 0 {
135			return FailureModeUnknown
136		}
137		return ""
138	}
139	
140	// containsAny reports whether s contains any of the given substrings. s is
141	// assumed to already be lower-cased by the caller.
142	func containsAny(s string, needles ...string) bool {
143		for _, n := range needles {
144			if n == "" {
145				continue
146			}
147			if strings.Contains(s, n) {
148				return true
149			}
150		}
151		return false
152	}
153	
154	// classifyLandingFailureMode computes the final failure_mode for a result
155	// after the orchestrator has made its landing decision. It prefers
156	// landing-specific signals (merge conflict, gate failure) over the worker's
157	// classification and falls back to workerMode for worker-level failures
158	// (agent execution failed, context overflow, etc.). Returns "" when the
159	// bead merged cleanly.
160	func classifyLandingFailureMode(landingOutcome, landingReason string, gateResults []GateCheckResult, workerMode string) string {
161		switch landingOutcome {
162		case "merged":
163			return ""
164		case "no-changes", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
165			return FailureModeNoChanges
166		case "no-evidence", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence:
167			return FailureModeNoEvidenceProduced
168		case "error":
169			if workerMode != "" {
170				return workerMode
171			}
172			return FailureModeUnknown
173		case "preserved":
174			switch landingReason {
175			case "merge conflict", "merge failed", "ff-merge not possible":
176				return FailureModeMergeConflict
177			case "post-run checks failed":
178				return classifyGateFailure(gateResults)
179			case RatchetPreserveReason:
180				return FailureModeRatchetMiss
181			}
182			// Other preserved reasons (e.g. "agent execution failed",
183			// "--no-merge specified") defer to the worker's classification.
184			if workerMode != "" {
185				return workerMode
186			}
187			return FailureModeUnknown
188		}
189		if workerMode != "" {
190			return workerMode
191		}
192		return FailureModeUnknown
193	}
194	
195	// classifyGateFailure inspects failed gate results to distinguish
196	// build_failure from test_failure. Gate definitions that fail to compile
197	// before running (go build, tsc, cargo build) typically surface build
198	// diagnostics in stderr; test runners surface test failure markers. When
199	// ambiguous, defaults to test_failure since the post-run checks gate is
200	// test-oriented by convention.
201	func classifyGateFailure(gateResults []GateCheckResult) string {
202		for _, gr := range gateResults {
203			if gr.Status != "fail" {
204				continue
205			}
206			combined := strings.ToLower(gr.Stdout + "\n" + gr.Stderr)
207			if containsAny(combined,
208				"build failed",
209				"compilation failed",
210				"compile error",
211				"compilation error",
212				"cannot find package",
213				"undefined:",
214				"undefined reference",
215				"syntax error") {
216				return FailureModeBuildFailure
217			}
218		}
219		return FailureModeTestFailure
220	}
221	
222	// Worker-level outcome constants — set by ExecuteBead() on the result.
223	// The parent orchestrator (LandBeadResult + ApplyLandingToResult) then
224	// overwrites Outcome and Status with the landing decision.
225	const (
226		ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded  = "task_succeeded"
227		ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed     = "task_failed"
228		ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges  = "task_no_changes"
229		ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence = "task_no_evidence"
230	)
231	
232	// Status constants — used by the loop and other consumers of ExecuteBeadReport.
233	// These map to specific close/unclaim behaviors in ExecuteBeadWorker.
234	const (
235		ExecuteBeadStatusStructuralValidationFailed = "structural_validation_failed"
236		ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed            = "execution_failed"
237		ExecuteBeadStatusPostRunCheckFailed         = "post_run_check_failed"
238		ExecuteBeadStatusRatchetFailed              = "ratchet_failed"
239		ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflict               = "land_conflict"
240		ExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed                 = "push_failed"
241		ExecuteBeadStatusPushConflict               = "push_conflict"
242		ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview       = "preserved_needs_review"
243		ExecuteBeadStatusNoEvidenceProduced         = "no_evidence_produced"
244		ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges                  = "no_changes"
245		ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied           = "already_satisfied"
246		ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess                    = "success"
247	
248		// ExecuteBeadStatusDeclinedNeedsDecomposition is a structured outcome
249		// signalling that the executor inspected the bead and concluded it cannot
250		// be delivered in a single pass — typically because the scope is
251		// epic-sized and must first be broken into sub-beads. It is distinct
252		// from no_changes (which means "I tried and there was nothing to change")
253		// and from execution_failed (which means the agent or harness errored).
254		//
255		// The loop treats this status as terminal-for-loop: it parks the bead
256		// with a long cooldown and records the recommended sub-beads as a
257		// structured `decomposition-recommendation` event. No further attempts
258		// happen until an operator clears the cooldown (e.g. via
259		// `ddx bead cooldown clear`) or splits the bead.
260		ExecuteBeadStatusDeclinedNeedsDecomposition = "declined_needs_decomposition"
261	
262		// Post-merge review outcomes. The bead was merged, then reviewed;
263		// the review returned a non-APPROVE verdict and the bead was reopened.
264		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewRequestChanges = "review_request_changes"
265		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewBlock          = "review_block"
266		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewMalfunction    = "review_malfunction"
267	
268		// ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictUnresolvable is set when execute-loop
269		// attempted 3-way ort auto-resolution and (if configured) a focused
270		// conflict-resolve agent run for a preserved iteration, and both failed.
271		// The bead is parked under LandConflictCooldown for operator review.
272		ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictUnresolvable = "land_conflict_unresolvable"
273	
274		// ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictNeedsHuman is set when the focused
275		// conflict-resolve agent escalated to BLOCKING — the conflict requires
276		// human judgment, not automated retry. Parked under the same short
277		// cooldown with a kind:land-conflict-needs-human event.
278		ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictNeedsHuman = "land_conflict_needs_human"
279	)
280	
281	// ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus maps a landing outcome to the supervisor-visible
282	// status contract. This is called by ApplyLandingToResult and by callers who
283	// build an ExecuteBeadReport from a landing result.
284	func ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(outcome string, exitCode int, reason string) string {
285		if exitCode != 0 {
286			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
287		}
288	
289		switch outcome {
290		case "merged":
291			// A merged outcome whose reason carries the push-failure marker
292			// means the local target branch advanced but `git push` rejected the
293			// new tip (large blob, branch protection, auth, divergence, etc.).
294			// Classify these as push_failed so the loop refuses to close the
295			// bead and operators are forced to investigate.
296			if strings.HasPrefix(reason, PushConflictReasonPrefix) {
297				return ExecuteBeadStatusPushConflict
298			}
299			if strings.HasPrefix(reason, PushFailedReasonPrefix) {
300				return ExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed
301			}
302			return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
303		case "no-changes", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
304			return ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges
305		case "no-evidence", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence:
306			return ExecuteBeadStatusNoEvidenceProduced
307		case "error":
308			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
309		case "preserved":
310			switch reason {
311			case "merge conflict", "merge failed", "ff-merge not possible":
312				return ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflict
313			case "post-run checks failed":
314				return ExecuteBeadStatusPostRunCheckFailed
315			case RatchetPreserveReason:
316				return ExecuteBeadStatusRatchetFailed
317			default:
318				if isPreservedNeedsReviewReason(reason) {
319					return ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview
320				}
321				// Preserved iterations may still be success when the caller
322				// explicitly requested no merge.
323				return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
324			}
325		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded:
326			return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
327		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed:
328			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
329		default:
330			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
331		}
332	}
333	
334	func isPreservedNeedsReviewReason(reason string) bool {
335		for _, prefix := range []string{
336			"large-deletion gate:",
337			"syntax sanity gate:",
338			"post-land gate failed:",
339		} {
340			if strings.HasPrefix(reason, prefix) {
341				return true
342			}
343		}
344		return false
345	}
346	
347	func ExecuteBeadStatusDetail(status, reason, errMsg string) string {
348		switch {
349		case reason != "" && errMsg != "" && reason != errMsg:
350			return reason + ": " + errMsg
351		case reason != "":
352			return reason
353		case errMsg != "":
354			return errMsg
355		case status != "":
356			return status
357		default:
358			return "unknown"
359		}
360	}

1	// Package routinglint implements the FEAT-006 routing-cleanup lint
2	// rule that mechanically forbids reintroduction of DDx-side
3	// "compensating routing" logic that was deleted with prejudice in
4	// ddx-3bd7396a.
5	//
6	// The parent epic ddx-fdd3ea36 §AC#7 says:
7	//
8	//	"Search DDx for code that re-implements upstream routing
9	//	concerns: allow-list checks, exact-pin filtering,
10	//	profile-to-preference mapping, provider cost scoring. Must be
11	//	absent on the default path. ... Dead-code detector run in CI
12	//	catches orphans."
13	//
14	// Parent-epic AC#7 originally expected the helpers to remain present,
15	// reachable only via --escalate. The follow-up bead ddx-3bd7396a
16	// instead deleted them entirely, so this analyzer enforces the
17	// stricter invariant: the listed compensating-routing identifiers and
18	// flag literals must not reappear anywhere in cli/ Go source. If they
19	// do, the cleanup has regressed and CI fails.
20	//
21	// Forbidden identifiers (exact name match):
22	//
23	//	ResolveProfileLadder, ResolveTierModelRef, AdaptiveMinTier,
24	//	workersByHarness, ResolveProfileLadderCallCount.
25	//
26	// Forbidden string literals (anywhere in cli/ Go source):
27	//
28	//	"--escalate", "--override-model",
29	//	"profile_ladders", "model_overrides",
30	//	"agent.routing.profile_ladders",
31	//	"agent.routing.model_overrides".
32	//
33	// String-literal occurrences inside this analyzer's own package and
34	// inside testdata fixtures are exempt.
35	//
36	// A specific occurrence may be intentionally exempted (e.g. the
37	// migration code that rejects a config containing
38	// agent.routing.profile_ladders has to name the literal key) by
39	// adding a
40	//
41	//	// routinglint:legacy-rejection reason="..."
42	//
43	// comment on the same line as the offending node, or on a comment
44	// line directly above it. The reason= clause must be present and
45	// non-empty.
46	package routinglint
47	
48	import (
49		"go/ast"
50		"go/token"
51		"regexp"
52		"strings"
53	
54		"golang.org/x/tools/go/analysis"
55		"golang.org/x/tools/go/analysis/passes/inspect"
56		"golang.org/x/tools/go/ast/inspector"
57	)
58	
59	const exemptionAnnotation = "routinglint:legacy-rejection"
60	
61	var exemptionRE = regexp.MustCompile(`routinglint:legacy-rejection\s+reason="[^"]+"`)
62	
63	// Analyzer is the FEAT-006 routing-cleanup analyzer.
64	var Analyzer = &analysis.Analyzer{
65		Name:     "routinglint",
66		Doc:      "flags reintroduction of compensating DDx-side routing helpers and flags retired by FEAT-006 / ddx-3bd7396a",
67		Requires: []*analysis.Analyzer{inspect.Analyzer},
68		Run:      run,
69	}
70	
71	// forbiddenIdents is the closed list of compensating-routing
72	// identifiers retired by ddx-3bd7396a. Reintroduction is a CI failure.
73	var forbiddenIdents = map[string]string{
74		"ResolveProfileLadder":          "tier-ladder resolver retired by ddx-3bd7396a — DDx must not re-implement profile-to-preference mapping",
75		"ResolveTierModelRef":           "tier-model resolver retired by ddx-3bd7396a — DDx must not re-implement exact-pin filtering",
76		"ResolveProfileLadderCallCount": "test seam for the retired tier-ladder resolver — also retired by ddx-3bd7396a",
77		"AdaptiveMinTier":               "adaptive-min-tier promotion logic retired by ddx-3bd7396a",
78		"workersByHarness":              "escalation worker grouping helper retired by ddx-3bd7396a",
79	}
80	
81	// forbiddenLiterals is the closed list of CLI flag and config-key
82	// strings retired by ddx-3bd7396a. They must not appear as Go string
83	// literals anywhere in cli/.
84	var forbiddenLiterals = map[string]string{
85		"--escalate":                    "--escalate flag retired by ddx-3bd7396a — escalation is not a DDx-side concern",
86		"--override-model":              "--override-model flag retired by ddx-3bd7396a — model overrides go through --model on the default path",
87		"profile_ladders":               "agent.routing.profile_ladders config key retired by ddx-3bd7396a",
88		"model_overrides":               "agent.routing.model_overrides config key retired by ddx-3bd7396a",
89		"agent.routing.profile_ladders": "agent.routing.profile_ladders config key retired by ddx-3bd7396a",
90		"agent.routing.model_overrides": "agent.routing.model_overrides config key retired by ddx-3bd7396a",
91	}
92	
93	// selfPkgFragments lists path fragments that mark this analyzer's own
94	// package or its testdata fixtures. Diagnostics from those packages
95	// are suppressed because the closed-list constants above legitimately
96	// embed every forbidden token.
97	var selfPkgFragments = []string{
98		"/tools/lint/routinglint",
99		"tools/lint/routinglint",
100	}
101	
102	func inSelfPkg(pass *analysis.Pass) bool {
103		if pass.Pkg == nil {
104			return false
105		}
106		p := pass.Pkg.Path()
107		for _, f := range selfPkgFragments {
108			if strings.Contains(p, f) {
109				return true
110			}
111		}
112		return false
113	}
114	
115	func run(pass *analysis.Pass) (interface{}, error) {
116		if inSelfPkg(pass) {
117			return nil, nil
118		}
119	
120		insp := pass.ResultOf[inspect.Analyzer].(*inspector.Inspector)
121	
122		// Build a per-file index of lines that carry an exemption
123		// annotation, either as an inline comment on the same line or as
124		// a stand-alone comment line directly above. The annotation
125		// covers the line it sits on plus the line immediately following.
126		exempted := make(map[token.Position]bool)
127		for _, f := range pass.Files {
128			for _, cg := range f.Comments {
129				for _, c := range cg.List {
130					if !strings.Contains(c.Text, exemptionAnnotation) {
131						continue
132					}
133					if !exemptionRE.MatchString(c.Text) {
134						pass.Report(analysis.Diagnostic{
135							Pos:     c.Pos(),
136							Message: `routinglint: ` + exemptionAnnotation + ` annotation is missing a non-empty reason="..." clause`,
137						})
138						continue
139					}
140					cp := pass.Fset.Position(c.Pos())
141					exempted[token.Position{Filename: cp.Filename, Line: cp.Line}] = true
142					exempted[token.Position{Filename: cp.Filename, Line: cp.Line + 1}] = true
143				}
144			}
145		}
146	
147		isExempt := func(pos token.Pos) bool {
148			p := pass.Fset.Position(pos)
149			return exempted[token.Position{Filename: p.Filename, Line: p.Line}]
150		}
151	
152		report := func(pos token.Pos, msg string) {
153			if isExempt(pos) {
154				return
155			}
156			pass.Report(analysis.Diagnostic{Pos: pos, Message: msg})
157		}
158	
159		// Forbidden identifiers — flag at every occurrence, both
160		// declarations and references. Matching is exact on Ident.Name so
161		// substrings inside larger identifiers (e.g. the test name
162		// TestExecuteLoopLocalRejectsProfileLadders) do not trip the lint.
163		insp.Preorder([]ast.Node{(*ast.Ident)(nil)}, func(n ast.Node) {
164			id := n.(*ast.Ident)
165			if reason, ok := forbiddenIdents[id.Name]; ok {
166				report(id.Pos(), "routinglint: forbidden identifier "+id.Name+" — "+reason)
167			}
168		})
169	
170		// Forbidden string literals — flag the literal node itself.
171		insp.Preorder([]ast.Node{(*ast.BasicLit)(nil)}, func(n ast.Node) {
172			lit := n.(*ast.BasicLit)
173			if lit.Kind != token.STRING {
174				return
175			}
176			// Trim surrounding quote/backtick so we compare raw value.
177			s := lit.Value
178			if len(s) >= 2 && (s[0] == '"' || s[0] == '`') {
179				s = s[1 : len(s)-1]
180			}
181			if reason, ok := forbiddenLiterals[s]; ok {
182				report(lit.Pos(), "routinglint: forbidden string literal "+lit.Value+" — "+reason)
183			}
184		})
185	
186		return nil, nil
187	}
188

1	// Package violations is the routinglint analyzer-test stub. Each
2	// declaration or reference below introduces exactly one forbidden
3	// pattern; the wanted-diagnostic comment on the same line is pinned
4	// by the analysistest framework.
5	package violations
6	
7	// Pattern A: forbidden identifier as a function declaration name.
8	
9	func ResolveProfileLadder() string { return "" } // want `routinglint: forbidden identifier ResolveProfileLadder`
10	
11	func ResolveTierModelRef() string { return "" } // want `routinglint: forbidden identifier ResolveTierModelRef`
12	
13	func ResolveProfileLadderCallCount() int { return 0 } // want `routinglint: forbidden identifier ResolveProfileLadderCallCount`
14

73	// NewTestConfigForRun returns a *Config that, when Resolve()d with the
74	// matching CLIOverrides, produces a ResolvedConfig whose run-path
75	// accessors return the values supplied in opts. Pure CLI-override
76	// fields (Provider, ModelRef, Effort) have no durable home on
77	// AgentConfig and must be applied at Resolve time via CLIOverrides.
78	func NewTestConfigForRun(opts TestRunConfigOpts) *Config {
79		return &Config{
80			Version: "1.0",
81			Agent: &AgentConfig{
82				Harness:       opts.Harness,
83				Model:         opts.Model,
84				TimeoutMS:     int(opts.Timeout / time.Millisecond),
85				SessionLogDir: opts.SessionLogDir,
86				Permissions:   opts.Permissions,
87			},
88		}
89	}
90	
91	// TestBeadConfigOpts names every durable knob the execute-bead worker
92	// reads via ResolvedConfig (per SD-024 §ExecuteBeadOptions →
93	// ExecuteBeadRuntime field classification). Tests must specify each
94	// field explicitly; there are no zero-value defaults that silently
95	// bypass real config.
96	//
97	// See SD-024 / TD-024 §Test config constructors and §Stage 3.
98	type TestBeadConfigOpts struct {
99		Harness  string
100		Model    string
101		Provider string // CLI-only override, no durable home on AgentConfig
102		ModelRef string // CLI-only override, no durable home on AgentConfig
103		Effort   string // CLI-only override, no durable home on AgentConfig
104		Mirror   *ExecutionsMirrorConfig
105	}
106	
107	// NewTestConfigForBead returns a *Config that, when Resolve()d with
108	// the matching CLIOverrides, produces a ResolvedConfig whose
109	// execute-bead-relevant accessors return the values supplied in opts.
110	// Pure CLI-override fields (Provider, ModelRef, Effort) have no
111	// durable home on AgentConfig and must be applied at Resolve time via
112	// CLIOverrides.
113	func NewTestConfigForBead(opts TestBeadConfigOpts) *Config {
114		cfg := &Config{
115			Version: "1.0",
116			Agent: &AgentConfig{
117				Harness: opts.Harness,
118				Model:   opts.Model,
119			},
120		}
121		if opts.Mirror != nil {
122			cfg.Executions = &ExecutionsConfig{Mirror: opts.Mirror}
123		}
124		return cfg
125	}
126	
127	// TestBeadOverrides returns the CLIOverrides that, combined with the
128	// *Config produced by NewTestConfigForBead(opts), drive a Resolve call
129	// to a ResolvedConfig matching opts. Pure-override fields (Provider,
130	// ModelRef, Effort) have no durable home on *Config; they are applied
131	// at Resolve time only.
132	func TestBeadOverrides(opts TestBeadConfigOpts) CLIOverrides {
133		return CLIOverrides{
134			Provider: opts.Provider,
135			ModelRef: opts.ModelRef,
136			Effort:   opts.Effort,
137		}
138	}
139	
140	// TestLoopOverrides returns the CLIOverrides that, combined with the
141	// *Config produced by NewTestConfigForLoop(opts), drive a Resolve call
142	// to a ResolvedConfig matching opts. Pure-override fields (Assignee,
143	// Profile, MinTier, MaxTier) have no durable home on *Config; they are
144	// applied at Resolve time only.
145	func TestLoopOverrides(opts TestLoopConfigOpts) CLIOverrides {
146		return CLIOverrides{
147			Assignee: opts.Assignee,
148			Profile:  opts.Profile,
149			MinTier:  opts.MinTier,
150			MaxTier:  opts.MaxTier,
151		}
152	}

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(agent): implement passthrough envelope for harness/provider/model [ddx-20047dd5]

Introduce AgentPassthrough as a typed envelope carrying harness/provider/model
as an opaque unit from CLI through to ServiceExecuteRequest. DDx no longer
inspects these values outside evidence recording and request construction.

- Add AgentPassthrough struct in config package (avoids circular imports)
- Populate and seal passthrough in Resolve(); expose via Passthrough() accessor
- executeOnService reads Harness/Provider/Model exclusively from Passthrough()
- Remove DDx-side provider→harness fallback (upstream routing concern)
- executeBeadRequested records passthrough fields separately from MinPower/MaxPower
- Add FailureModeBlockedByPassthroughConstraint and FailureModeAgentPowerUnsatisfied
  with pattern matching at the top of ClassifyFailureMode
- routinglint: forbid queueByHarness, retryByPassthrough, endpointByPassthrough,
  catalogThresholdForHarness to enforce the envelope contract (AC8)
- Tests: AC3 (envelope invariant under power escalation), AC6 (invalid passthrough
  not pre-validated by DDx), AC7 (conflict modes distinct from generic failures)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
